tools.name: add_option_to_watchlist
tools.description: Add option contracts to the user's options watchlist. Works for both equity options (AAPL, NVDA) and index options (SPX, NDX, RUT). Source option_ids from get_option_instruments. Confirm with the user before calling — this is a real write.

option_ids.description: Option contract UUIDs to add. Each becomes a single-leg position on the user's options watchlist. Source from get_option_instruments.

position_type.description: "long" (default) or "short". Applies to every option_id in this call. For mixed long/short adds, issue two calls.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

list_id.description: The options watchlist this call resolved to and targeted.

option_ids.description: Option contract UUIDs this call targeted, echoed for the caller's reference.

position_type.description: The position_type applied to every contract in this call.

status.description: "ok" when the upstream accepted the operation.

guide.description: Each option_id is added as a single-leg position with the supplied position_type (defaults to "long"). "no options watchlist found" in the error means the user has not enabled options trading — surface that to the user rather than retrying. Re-adding a contract that is already on the list is a no-op.

tools.name: add_to_watchlist
tools.description: Add items to a watchlist. Exactly one of symbols (stocks/ETFs), currency_pair_ids (crypto), or index_ids (market indexes like SPX, NDX) is required — mutually exclusive. For options use add_option_to_watchlist (separate dedicated watchlist). Futures still require the Robinhood app. Already-present items are no-ops. Confirm with the user before calling.

currency_pair_ids.description: Currency-pair UUIDs to add (e.g. the object_id from get_watchlist_items where object_type=currency_pair, or the id from get_currency_pairs). Mutually exclusive with symbols and index_ids.

index_ids.description: Market-index UUIDs to add (the id field from get_indexes; SPX, NDX, DJI, etc.). Mutually exclusive with symbols and currency_pair_ids.

list_id.description: UUID of the watchlist to add items to.

symbols.description: Stock symbols to add (e.g. ['AAPL', 'NVDA']). US stocks and ETFs only. Mutually exclusive with currency_pair_ids and index_ids.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

currency_pair_ids.description: The currency-pair UUIDs this call targeted (crypto path).

index_ids.description: The index UUIDs this call targeted (index path).

object_type.description: 'instrument' when symbols were used, 'currency_pair' when currency_pair_ids were used, 'index' when index_ids were used.

status.description: 'ok' when the upstream accepted the operation.

symbols.description: The stock symbols this call targeted (instrument path).

guide.description: On success the response echoes the operations applied. 400 with 'invalid instrument id' means a symbol could not be resolved — confirm spelling with the user. 404 means the list_id is not writable by the user (likely a curated list; use a custom list instead). 400 with an allowed_object_types message means the target list does not accept this asset type — check the list's allowed_object_types in get_watchlists.

tools.name: cancel_equity_order
tools.description: Cancel an open equity order by order_id. Always confirm with the user before calling. Resolve order_id via get_equity_orders if the user refers to it by symbol or description; pass the same account_number. Requires an agentic_allowed=true account; non-agentic accounts are rejected — do not call. Cancellation may be rejected if the order has already filled, was already cancelled, or is otherwise ineligible.

account_number.description: Brokerage account that owns the order. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts. Must be agentic_allowed=true. The upstream rejects mismatches against the order's owning account.

order_id.description: Order UUID from get_equity_orders. Must live in account_number.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

accepted.description: True when the broker accepted the cancel request.

guide.description: accepted=true means the broker accepted the cancel request, NOT that the order is already cancelled (cancellation is asynchronous). For the final state, call get_equity_orders: state 'pending_cancelled' = in flight; 'cancelled' or 'partially_filled_rest_cancelled' = succeeded; any filled state = a fill raced the cancel. Offer to check the final state. On error, the cancel was rejected outright — report verbatim.

tools.name: cancel_option_order
tools.description: Cancel an open option order by account_number + order_id. Always confirm with the user before calling. Resolve order_id via get_option_orders if the user refers to it by description; pass the same account_number you used there. Requires an agentic_allowed=true account; non-agentic accounts are rejected. Cancellation may be rejected if the order has already filled, was already cancelled, or is otherwise ineligible.

account_number.description: Brokerage account that owns the order. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts. Must be agentic_allowed=true. Mismatches against the order's owning account are rejected.

order_id.description: Order UUID from get_option_orders. Must live in account_number.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

accepted.description: True when the broker accepted the cancel request.

guide.description: accepted=true means the broker accepted the cancel request, NOT that the order is already cancelled (cancellation is asynchronous). For the final state, call get_option_orders: state 'pending_cancelled' = in flight; 'cancelled' = succeeded; any filled state = a fill raced the cancel. Offer to check the final state. On error, the cancel was rejected outright — report verbatim.

tools.name: create_scan
tools.description:
Create a new saved scanner (screener) on the user's account, optionally applying a preset and custom filters in a single call. Returns the new scan's id, title, applied filters, and the initial live market results.

This tool composes multiple Beacon operations:
  1. Create an empty scan
  2. If a non-INITIAL preset was requested: apply that preset configuration (DAILY_GAINERS, DAILY_LOSERS, HIGH_OPTIONS_VOLUME_IV, UPCOMING_EARNINGS)
  3. If custom filters were provided: apply them (replaces any preset filters)
  4. If a custom title was provided: set the title

If any step after the initial create fails, the scan still exists with the partial state — the response surfaces what was applied. Use update_scan_filters / update_scan_config to fix anything that failed.

Parameters:
- preset (optional) — starting preset. Default: DAILY_GAINERS when no filters supplied, INITIAL when filters supplied. Valid values: INITIAL, DAILY_GAINERS, DAILY_LOSERS, HIGH_OPTIONS_VOLUME_IV, UPCOMING_EARNINGS.
- filters (optional) — array of custom filter specs. Call get_scanner_filter_specs to discover valid filter_type / predicate values.
- title (optional) — custom human-readable name for the scan.

Example: to make "stocks with RSI > 70 and volume > 1M, sorted by volume desc", call with
  preset = "INITIAL"
  filters = [
    {"filter_type": "FILTER_TYPE_RSI", "predicate": ">", "values": ["70"], "interval": "1d", "length": 14},
    {"filter_type": "FILTER_TYPE_VOLUME", "predicate": ">", "values": ["1000000"], "interval": "1d"}
  ]
  title = "High RSI + High Volume"

filters.description: Custom filters to apply after the preset. Each filter has filter_type (FILTER_TYPE_... enum), predicate (>, <, =, BETWEEN, etc.), values, optional interval (e.g. 1d), and optional length (e.g. 14 for RSI). Call get_scanner_filter_specs first for valid filter_type / predicate combinations.

filter_type.description: Wire-format enum name, e.g. "FILTER_TYPE_RSI". See the scanner-filter-specs resource for valid values.

interval.description: Time granularity for time-series filters (e.g. "1d"). Use one of the supported_intervals from the filter's scanner-filter-specs entry.

length.description: Lookback length for filters that need one (e.g. RSI period of 14). Use one of the supported_lengths from the filter's scanner-filter-specs entry.

plot.description: Plot / price-field input for filters that have one (e.g. "open" or "close" for % Change). Use one of the supported_plots from the filter's scanner-filter-specs entry.

predicate.description: Wire-format enum name, e.g. "PREDICATE_GREATER_THAN". See the scanner-filter-specs resource for the predicates supported by each filter.

values.description: Threshold values. Single-element for unary predicates, two-element for BETWEEN, multi-element for IN_LIST/ANY_OF.

preset.description: Starting preset for the new scan. One of: INITIAL (no preset; only valid if filters are provided), DAILY_GAINERS, DAILY_LOSERS, HIGH_OPTIONS_VOLUME_IV, UPCOMING_EARNINGS. Defaults to DAILY_GAINERS when no filters are provided, INITIAL when filters are provided.

title.description: Optional custom title for the saved scan. If omitted, Beacon assigns a default title based on the preset.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

result.description: The newly-created scan plus its initial live results.

cortex_managed.description: True when this scan was created or modified by Cortex. Read-only via MCP tools.

filters_applied.description: Filters in effect when the scan was run. Always populated on create_scan / update_scan_filters responses; optional on run_scan.

filter_type.description: Filter category in human form (e.g. "RSI", "Volume", "Market Cap"). See the scanner-filter-specs resource for the full list.

interval.description: Time granularity for time-series filters (e.g. "1d", "1h"). Empty for snapshot filters.

length.description: Lookback length for filters that need one (e.g. RSI period of 14). Zero/omitted for filters that don't take a length.

plot.description: Plot / price-field input for filters that have one (e.g. % Change uses "open" or "close" to choose changeFromOpen vs changeFromClose). Empty for filters that don't take a plot.

values.description: Threshold values for the predicate. Single-element for >/</=, two-element for BETWEEN, multi-element for IN_LIST/ANY_OF.

results.description: Matched instruments, in the scan's current sort order. Live at time of execution.

columns.description: Column display name -> cell value, for every visible column in the scan's configuration. Numeric values are upstream-formatted strings; the agent should re-format for the user.

instrument_id.description: Robinhood instrument UUID. Pass to other trading tools that take an instrument_id.

instrument_type.description: Asset class: STOCK, ETF, etc. Mirrors hippo.model.v1.InstrumentType.

ticker.description: Trading symbol (e.g. AAPL). Sourced from the scan's symbol column; empty when the scan has no symbol column visible.

scan_id.description: Scan identifier. Stable across reads and writes.

sorted_by.description: Current sort column + direction in human form (e.g. "Volume desc"). Empty when no sort is configured.

total_items.description: Total instruments matching the scan's filters. May exceed len(results) when the scan's frontend_limit is hit.

guide.description:
How to interpret the response:
- result.scan_id — newly created scan. Pass this to run_scan, update_scan_filters, or update_scan_config in follow-up calls.
- result.scan_title — final title (custom if provided, else Beacon's default for the preset).
- result.filters_applied — the filter set actually in effect after the call. If this doesn't match what the user asked for, one of the composite steps failed silently — surface that to the user.
- result.results — initial live results (real-time market data at scan creation time).
- result.total_items — total matches. May exceed len(results) if the scan's frontend_limit is hit.

When presenting:
- Confirm the scan was created and note its title + filters_applied (so the user knows what was actually saved).
- Render the initial results as a table.
- Tell the user the scan is saved and can be re-run anytime with run_scan + the returned scan_id.
- If results is empty: tell the user no instruments match the filters right now, and suggest broadening them with update_scan_filters.

Error handling:
- "unknown preset X" → preset enum not recognized. List the valid presets to the user.
- "filter[N]: ..." → filter validation failure at index N. Tell the user which filter and why. The scan may still exist with just the preset applied — they can fix the filter with update_scan_filters.
- Partial-failure responses (e.g. "scan X created but preset Y could not be applied") indicate a partial success: the scan exists but is missing some of the intended state. Use filters_applied on the response to see what actually landed; mention this clearly to the user so they know whether they need to follow up with update_scan_filters.

tools.name: create_watchlist
tools.description: Create a new custom watchlist for the user. Confirm the name with the user before calling — this is a real write. Do not use this to follow a Robinhood-curated list (use follow_watchlist).

display_description.description: Short description shown under the name.

display_name.description: Name for the new watchlist (e.g. 'Tech Stocks'). Must be unique among the user's watchlists.

icon_emoji.description: Emoji shown next to the name (one character).

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

watchlist.description: The newly created or updated watchlist.

allowed_object_types.description: Which kinds of objects can be added to this list. Common values: instrument (US stock/ETF), currency_pair (crypto), index (market index), option_strategy (options watchlist — see add_option_to_watchlist), futures. add_to_watchlist supports instrument, currency_pair, and index; options use the dedicated add_option_to_watchlist tool; futures still require the Robinhood app.

display_description.description: Optional short description shown under the name.

icon_emoji.description: Emoji shown alongside the name. Present on custom lists; absent on Robinhood-curated lists.

id.description: List UUID. Pass to other watchlist tools (get_watchlist_items, add/remove_from_watchlist, update_watchlist) as list_id.

item_count.description: Number of items in the list.

owner_type.description: 'custom' means a user-created list. 'robinhood' means a curated list the user is following. Use this to distinguish user-owned lists (writable: rename, delete items, add items) from curated lists (read + follow/unfollow only).

guide.description: On success the response includes the new list_id — pass it to add_to_watchlist to populate the list. If a 400 mentions 'unique set' the user already has a list with that name; suggest a different name.

tools.name: follow_watchlist
tools.description: Follow a Robinhood-curated list so it appears in the user's watchlists. Confirm with the user before calling. Use only for curated lists; the user already owns their custom lists.

list_id.description: UUID of the Robinhood-curated list to follow. Obtain from get_popular_watchlists.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

follower.description: The new or pre-existing follower record. Omitted for unfollow which has no response body.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the user first followed this list. The follower endpoint is idempotent — re-following an already-followed list returns the existing record, so this timestamp may be older than 'just now'.

list_id.description: UUID of the list that was followed.

owner_type.description: Always 'robinhood' for follow_watchlist — only Robinhood-curated lists are followable.

user_id.description: UUID of the user that followed the list (always the caller).

status.description: 'ok' when the upstream accepted the operation.

guide.description: 400 with 'you cannot follow a list you own' means the user already owns this list (no action needed). 400 with a follow-limit message means the user is at their max followed lists — they need to unfollow one first.

tools.name: get_accounts
tools.description: List the user's brokerage accounts. Use this to look up account_number values needed by other tools. If the user has multiple accounts and hasn't specified which one, present the list and ask them to choose. Does NOT return reliable buying power — route buying-power questions through get_portfolio.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

accounts.description: The user's brokerage accounts. Empty when the user has none.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number; pass to any tool that requires account_number. Mask all but the last 4 digits (e.g. '••••1234') when displaying to the user.

affiliate.description: Affiliate / region code (e.g. 'RHF', 'RHUK', 'RHSG'). Affects feature availability for the account.

agentic_allowed.description: True when this account is accessible to this agent specifically. An account with false may be agentic-enabled but scoped to a different agent type — do not tell the user it is disabled. Sole source of truth for eligibility — never infer it from nickname text. review_*/place_*/cancel_* tools reject accounts where this is false.

deactivated.description: True when the account is currently deactivated.

is_default.description: True for the user's default account. Surface when choosing among multiple accounts.

management_type.description: Management classification (e.g. 'managed') when applicable; layered on top of brokerage_account_type rather than replacing it.

nickname.description: User-set nickname; present alongside account_number when set. Cosmetic only — not an agentic_allowed or agentic-account signal.

option_level.description: Options approval: 'option_level_2' (long calls/puts, covered calls, cash-secured puts), 'option_level_3' (spreads and complex strategies), or empty/'option_level_0' (not approved).

permanently_deactivated.description: True when the account is permanently closed (cannot be reactivated).

rhc_account_number.description: Crypto account number for this account (e.g. 'RHC1234567'). Absent when no linked crypto account exists. Mask all but the last 4 digits (e.g. '••••1234') when displaying to the user.

rhs_account_number.description: Numeric brokerage account number — pass this (not account_number) to any nummus-backed tool that asks for an account identifier.

state.description: Account state — 'active' is normal; anything else (deactivated, restricted, etc.) means the account cannot trade normally.

type.description: 'cash' or 'margin' — the trading-permissions classification for the brokerage account. Crypto accounts are never marginable, so a linked crypto account has no equivalent. On retirement (IRA) accounts 'margin' means limited margin; full margin is not available there.

guide.description: Sort the list deterministically when presenting: the default account first, agentic accounts (agentic_allowed=true) second, then other individual accounts, then retirement (IRA) accounts. Present account_number, rhc_account_number (label it "Crypto Account"), brokerage_account_type (label it "Account Type" — a linked crypto account always shares it, so never add a separate crypto account type column), type (label it "Brokerage Trading Type"), nickname (if set), and is_default — in that column order. In the Crypto Account column show "N/A" for accounts with an empty rhc_account_number; omit the column entirely only when no account has one. When referring to the account in user-facing prose (e.g. "your <X> account"), use nickname when set; fall back to brokerage_account_type otherwise. When displaying account_number or rhc_account_number to the user, mask all but the last 4 digits (e.g. '••••1234'); pass the full value to other tools unchanged — including get_option_level_upgrade_info and any fallback upgrade URL; masking breaks the link. Flag agentic_allowed when the user is choosing an account for a trade. agentic_allowed is caller-relative — true means accessible to this agent, false means this agent cannot act on it (the account may still be agentic-enabled for a different agent). Do not use nickname to determine agentic-account eligibility. Skip false accounts for trade operations; if the user asks why, say the account is not accessible to this agent, not that it needs to be enabled. When option_level is empty or 'option_level_0' and the user wants options, call get_option_level_upgrade_info with the account_number; if that tool is unavailable, direct the user to https://applink.robinhood.com/upgrade_options?account_number=<that account's account_number> (embed the full unmasked account_number in the URL). management_type='managed' is layered on brokerage_account_type — surface alongside, not as a replacement. For tools that ask for an account identifier on a crypto-backed flow (get_crypto_positions, future crypto write tools), pass rhs_account_number; for everything else (equities, options, etc.) pass account_number.

tools.name: get_earnings_calendar
tools.description: List earnings reports scheduled across the market over a date window (up to 31 days), optionally limited to high-market-cap names. Returns one entry per report event — estimated/actual EPS, report date and timing (am/pm), and company-verification status. Use this for market-wide discovery ("what large-caps report this week?"). For a specific known ticker, use get_earnings_results instead. Read-only.

days.description: Window length in days, measured from start_date. Defaults to 7. Positive = forward window (e.g. 7 = the next 7 days, inclusive of start_date); negative = look-back window (e.g. -7 = the 7 days ending at start_date). Must be a non-zero value between -31 and 31 — windows wider than 31 days are rejected.

filter.description: Optional result filter. Set to 'high_market_cap' to limit the calendar to high-market-cap names (market cap over $1B) — useful for 'what large-caps report this week' style questions. Omit for all names.

start_date.description: Window anchor, YYYY-MM-DD. Defaults to today (US/Eastern) when omitted.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

results.description: Earnings report events in the window, one per company-quarter, sorted ascending by report date. Empty array when no reports fall in the window.

eps.description: Estimated and actual earnings per share for the quarter. Both sides may be null — see field docs.

actual.description: Reported EPS for the quarter. Null for upcoming quarters (not yet reported) — pair with report.date to tell the two apart.

estimate.description: Expected (estimated) EPS for the quarter. Null when no estimate is available for this quarter.

report.description: Report scheduling — date, am/pm timing, company-verification flag. Null when upstream has no scheduled event for this quarter yet.

date.description: YYYY-MM-DD report date. Present whenever the parent report block is non-null — use the parent's null/non-null state, not this field, to detect 'no scheduled event'.

timing.description: 'am' (before market) or 'pm' (after market). Null when timing is not yet known — only distinguishes before/after market, not an exact time.

verified.description: True when the company has confirmed the report date. False when the date is tentative (not yet company-confirmed).

symbol.description: Ticker symbol the earnings event belongs to.

guide.description: Each entry is one company's report event within the window, sorted ascending by report date. The window spans days from start_date (positive = forward, negative = look-back). Distinguish "upcoming" from "already reported" by eps.actual: null means not yet reported. When report.verified is false, present the date as tentative rather than confirmed. An empty results array means no reports fall in the window (with filter=high_market_cap, no high-market-cap names do) — not an error. This tool does NOT return an "expected move" — pair with options data for implied-volatility-based moves.

tools.name: get_earnings_results
tools.description: Get recent and upcoming earnings for ONE equity symbol — estimated/actual EPS, report date and timing (am/pm), and company-verification status. Returns the trailing up to 8 quarters. Use this for earnings-timing questions ("does AAPL report this week?"), EPS surprise analysis, and screening for upcoming earnings risk on a specific stock. For market-wide earnings calendar queries across many symbols, use get_earnings_calendar. Read-only.

symbol.description: Stock symbol to look up (one symbol per call). Exact-ticker match — no name or partial-ticker resolution. Lowercase and whitespace-padded input is normalized to uppercase-trimmed before forwarding. Returns the trailing up to 8 quarters of earnings for the symbol.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

not_found.description: Populated with the requested symbol (in canonical uppercase form) when upstream could not resolve it — typo, delisted, or non-equity ticker. Absent / null when the symbol resolved (even if it had zero earnings entries).

results.description: Earnings entries for the requested symbol, sorted ascending by quarter (historical first, upcoming last). Empty array when the symbol resolved but upstream had no entries in its retention window, OR when the symbol failed to resolve (in which case it appears in not_found).

eps.description: Estimated and actual earnings per share for the quarter. Both sides may be null — see field docs.

actual.description: Reported EPS for the quarter. Null for upcoming quarters (not yet reported) — pair with report.date to tell the two apart.

estimate.description: Expected (estimated) EPS for the quarter. Null when no estimate is available for this quarter.

report.description: Report scheduling — date, am/pm timing, company-verification flag. Null when upstream has no scheduled event for this quarter yet.

date.description: YYYY-MM-DD report date. Present whenever the parent report block is non-null — use the parent's null/non-null state, not this field, to detect 'no scheduled event'.

timing.description: 'am' (before market) or 'pm' (after market). Null when timing is not yet known — only distinguishes before/after market, not an exact time.

verified.description: True when the company has confirmed the report date. False when the date is tentative (not yet company-confirmed).

symbol.description: Ticker symbol the earnings event belongs to.

guide.description: Entries are sorted ascending — historical quarters first, most recent / upcoming quarter last. Distinguish "upcoming" from "reported" by eps.actual: null means the company has not yet reported. For "will earnings happen by date X" questions, use report.date. When report.verified is false, present the date as tentative rather than confirmed. If upstream cannot resolve the symbol (typo, delisted, non-equity), the response carries an empty results array and the symbol appears in not_found — surface that as "no data for X" rather than treating it as a tool failure. This tool does NOT return an "expected move" — pair with options data for implied-volatility-based moves.

tools.name: get_equity_fundamentals
tools.description: Get today's fundamentals for one or more stock symbols — valuation ratios (PE, P/B), capitalization (market cap, shares outstanding, float), today's session OHLCV, trailing volume averages, 52-week range, dividend schedule, and company profile. For real-time quotes use get_equity_quotes; for time-series price history use get_equity_historicals.

bounds.description: Trading session the day-level fields (open / high / low / volume / overnight_volume) are drawn from. One of 'regular' (regular trading hours), 'trading' (regular + post-market), 'extended' (pre-market + regular + post-market), '24_5' (24-hour, 5-day-trading-week). Does not affect valuation fields. overnight_volume only populates when bounds=24_5. Defaults to 'regular' when omitted.

symbols.description: One or more stock symbols (max 10 per call). Exact-ticker match — no name or partial-ticker resolution. Lowercase and whitespace-padded input is normalized to uppercase-trimmed before forwarding.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

not_found.description: Requested symbols that the upstream could not resolve (delisted, invalid, non-equity). Returned in canonical uppercase form. Absent / null when every requested symbol resolved.

results.description: One entry per resolved symbol, in upstream order. Empty if no symbol resolved. Symbols that did not resolve (delisted, invalid, non-equity) are NOT placeholder-null'd here — they appear in not_found instead so the whole call doesn't fail.

average_volume.description: Trailing 52-week average daily share volume (the unqualified name reflects the upstream contract).

bounds.description: Session-bounds tag the data was computed over (e.g. '24_5' = 24-hour, 5-day-trading-week).

description.description: Short company description in the caller's preferred language.

distribution_frequency.description: How often the dividend is paid (e.g. 'quarterly', 'monthly', 'annual'). Present only for dividend-paying instruments.

dividend_per_share.description: Most recent declared dividend amount per share. Present only for dividend-paying instruments.

dividend_yield.description: Trailing 12-month dividend yield as a percentage (1.85 means 1.85%). Null for non-dividend-paying instruments.

ex_dividend_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD next ex-dividend date — buy before this date to receive the upcoming dividend. Null for non-dividend stocks.

financial_status_description.description: Human-readable form of financial_status_indicator. Empty when the indicator is the 'no deficiency' value.

financial_status_indicator.description: Short code from the listing exchange flagging listing-deficiency status (e.g. 'CC0' = no deficiency, 'D' = deficient, 'E' = delinquent). Pair with financial_status_description for the human-readable form.

float.description: Publicly tradable share count (excludes insider / restricted holdings).

high_52_weeks.description: Highest price over the trailing 52 weeks.

high_52_weeks_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD date the trailing-52-week high was set.

low_52_weeks.description: Lowest price over the trailing 52 weeks.

low_52_weeks_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD date the trailing-52-week low was set.

market_cap.description: Market capitalization in USD. Null for delisted or zero-share-outstanding instruments.

market_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD trading date the snapshot is 'as of'.

overnight_volume.description: Overnight session share volume. Null unless bounds=24_5 was requested; treat null as 'not applicable to this session', not 'no overnight trades'.

payable_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD date the next dividend will be paid. Null for non-dividend stocks.

pb_ratio.description: Price-to-book ratio. Null for instruments with non-positive book value or no book-value data.

pe_ratio.description: Price-to-earnings ratio (TTM). Can be negative when earnings are negative. Null for recent IPOs or non-equity instruments.

record_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD date of record for the next dividend. Present only for dividend-paying instruments.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol.

thirty_day_sec_yield.description: 30-day SEC yield as a percentage (1.85 means 1.85%). Present primarily for bond funds; absent for individual stocks.

guide.description: Symbols that did not resolve (delisted, invalid, non-equity) appear in not_found instead of results; surface them to the user as "no data for X" rather than ignoring them. Present nulls as "not applicable" rather than zero. Pair financial_status_indicator with financial_status_description — never surface the indicator code alone.

tools.name: get_equity_historicals
tools.description:
Get OHLCV bars for one or more equity symbols across an explicit time range. Use this for charting, "recent activity" questions, and backtesting. The server auto-selects an interval when one is not provided. If the bar's interpolated field is true, bar was synthesized to fill a gap and carry no new information.

Parameter rules:
- interval is optional; when omitted, the server auto-selects an interval that targets ~2,500 bars across the requested range. Provide an explicit interval only when you need a specific granularity.
- interval values are fixed; the server does NOT aggregate intermediate bars. For a custom interval (e.g. 3-minute), request the next-finer fixed interval and aggregate client-side.
- bounds defaults to 'regular' (RTH only). Use 'extended' or '24_5' only when the user explicitly asks about extended-hours activity.
- adjustment_type defaults to 'split' (split-adjusted, the right default for backtesting). Use 'none' for raw prices, 'all' for split + dividend adjustment.
- If the range would produce more bars than the upstream allows at the explicitly requested interval, narrow the range or coarsen the interval — the call is rejected before reaching upstream. The cap does not apply when interval is auto-selected.

adjustment_type.description: Corporate-action adjustment: 'none' (raw prices), 'split' (default; right for backtesting), or 'all' (split + dividend; intraday only).

end_time.description: End of the range (RFC3339 UTC). Optional — when omitted, defaults to the current time.

interval.description: Bar interval. Optional — when omitted, the server picks an interval that targets ~2,500 bars across the requested range. Intraday: 15second, 30second, minute, 5minute, 10minute, 30minute, hour, 4hour. Interday: day, week, month, 3month, 6month, year, 5year, 10year, 20year, 50year. Note: the 1-minute bar is named 'minute' (not '1minute').

start_time.description: Start of the range (RFC3339 UTC, e.g. '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z'). Required.

symbols.description: One or more stock symbols (uppercase). Up to 10 per call.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

not_found.description: Requested symbols that did not resolve (delisted, invalid, or non-equity). Absent when every requested symbol resolved.

results.description: One entry per requested symbol that resolved upstream.

bars.description: Bars in ascending time order. Empty if no trading activity in the window.

begins_at.description: Start of the bar (RFC3339 UTC). Bars are left-edge labeled.

close_price.description: Last trade price in the bar. NOT the official settled close; for that use get_equity_quotes closes[].

high_price.description: Highest trade price in the bar.

interpolated.description: True when the bar was synthesized to fill a gap (no trades occurred). OHLC values are forward-filled from a nearby bar; volume is zero.

low_price.description: Lowest trade price in the bar.

open_price.description: First trade price in the bar.

session.description: Wire-format session: 'reg' (regular hours), 'pre' (pre-market), or 'post' (after-hours). Empty/absent when the bar's session is unknown.

volume.description: Share volume in the bar. Zero for interpolated bars.

bounds.description: Session bounds echoed from the request.

interval.description: Bar interval. Echoes the request when one was supplied; otherwise reports the server-auto-selected interval.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol.

guide.description: Bars are left-edge labeled in UTC; convert to the user's timezone for presentation. Treat interpolated=true bars as gap-fill — they carry no new info and should usually be hidden in charts and ignored for analytics. close_price on the most recent bar is NOT the official settled close — use get_equity_quotes for that. For a multi-bar move, compute return from the first bar's open_price to the last bar's close_price; do not aggregate intermediate interpolated bars. When bounds='regular' the bar series covers only regular trading hours — gaps between trading sessions are not zero-filled, they are simply absent. When the request omitted interval, the 'interval' field on each result reports what the server auto-selected.

tools.name: get_equity_orders
tools.description:
Fetch equity orders for an account — list mode (newest first; open and closed, including fills, cancellations, rejections) or single-order mode by passing order_id. When the user asks for "orders" without specifying equity or options, call both get_equity_orders and get_option_orders in parallel.

Filtering tips:
- Prefer narrow queries: combine state, symbol, and/or created_at_gte for specific questions (e.g. "my filled AAPL orders this week") — the per-page cap is fixed.
- created_at_gte: interpret relative times in the user's timezone, convert to UTC before sending.
- symbol forces a symbol→instrument lookup; omit it if you don't need it.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts.

created_at_gte.description: Lower bound (inclusive). ISO 8601 UTC or YYYY-MM-DD; naive values are interpreted as UTC.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor. Omit for the first page; for the next page, pass the cursor query param from the prior response's next URL.

order_id.description: Filter to a single order by UUID. The response shape is unchanged (orders[] with at most one entry); empty when the order does not belong to account_number.

state.description: Filter by single state: new, queued, confirmed, unconfirmed, partially_filled, filled, cancelled, rejected, failed, voided.

symbol.description: Filter to one symbol (triggers a symbol→instrument lookup before the orders call).

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

next.description: Next-page URL. Empty when there are no more pages.

orders.description: Equity orders on this page, newest first. Empty when the cursor has no rows.

average_price.description: Weighted average fill price across executions. Null until the first fill.

cumulative_quantity.description: Shares filled so far. Less than quantity while partially filled; equal once filled.

dollar_based_amount.description: Notional amount for dollar-based market orders; null for share-quantity orders.

currency_code.description: ISO currency code; always 'USD' for equity orders today.

executions.description: Per-fill detail; reference only for per-lot questions.

quantity.description: Shares filled in this execution.

instrument_id.description: Instrument UUID; resolve to symbol via the instruments endpoint if needed.

last_transaction_at.description: Time of the most recent fill, cancel, or reject. Null on a brand-new unfilled order.

placed_agent.description: Source that placed the order: 'user' (app), 'agentic' (MCP), 'recurring', 'drip', 'brokeback' (system), or other internal values.

price.description: Limit price for limit/stop_limit orders; null for market orders.

quantity.description: Originally-requested share quantity. Null for dollar-based orders before the first fill.

state.description: Lifecycle state: new, queued, unconfirmed, partially_filled, filled, cancelled, rejected, failed, voided, pending_cancelled, partially_filled_rest_cancelled, and (for short-sale orders) locating, locate_failed.

stop_price.description: Stop trigger price for stop_market/stop_limit orders; null otherwise.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol.

type.description: Upstream type: 'market' or 'limit'. Combine with trigger to recover the user-facing market/limit/stop_market/stop_limit.

guide.description: For dollar-based orders (dollar_based_amount populated, quantity sometimes null) describe in dollars until cumulative_quantity is meaningful. Mention placed_agent when it is not 'user'. Recover the user-facing order type from type+trigger or from price/stop_price. Present symbol, side, type, state, quantity (or dollar_based_amount), cumulative_quantity when partially filled, price for limit orders, average_price once filled, last_transaction_at (or created_at), and id when cancellation may follow; suppress other fields unless asked.

tools.name: get_equity_positions
tools.description: List open equity positions for a specific brokerage account. Returns symbol, quantity, average cost, and per-position hold breakdowns.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor. Omit for the first page; for the next page, pass the cursor query param from the prior response's next URL.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

next.description: Next-page URL. Empty when there are no more pages.

positions.description: Open equity positions on this page. Empty when the cursor has no rows.

average_buy_price.description: Average cost per share as shown in the Robinhood app; reflects partial sells. Omitted for positions still reconciling.

intraday_quantity.description: Portion of quantity filled today (upstream is timezone-aware).

quantity.description: Total shares held, including today's fills. Negative for short positions.

shares_available_for_sells.description: Shares the user can sell right now (server-computed after subtracting holds).

shares_held_for_asset_transfer.description: Shares locked by an outgoing asset transfer (e.g. ACATS-out).

shares_held_for_options_events.description: Shares earmarked for an upcoming options exercise/assignment.

shares_held_for_sells.description: Shares locked by open outgoing sell orders.

shares_held_for_stock_grants.description: Shares held for settlement of stock grants / unsettled inbound fills.

shares_pending_from_options_events.description: Shares pending from an options exercise/assignment, not yet booked into quantity.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol.

guide.description: Sellable shares: use shares_available_for_sells, not quantity. Yesterday's quantity = quantity - intraday_quantity. average_buy_price is the average cost per share shown in the Robinhood app (it already reflects partial sells) — use it as the user's average cost; it may be omitted for positions still reconciling. No market price here — for current value or PnL, call get_equity_quotes and multiply by quantity. Present symbol, quantity, average_buy_price, and type when not 'long'; suppress other fields unless asked.

tools.name: get_equity_price_book
tools.description: Get a real-time bid/ask order book (Level 2) snapshot for one or more equity symbols (max 4), showing the ladder of price levels and resting share size on each side. Use to read supply/demand depth before entering or exiting a position.

symbols.description: One or more stock symbols, max 4 per call.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

books.description: One order-book snapshot per successfully fetched symbol, in request order. A symbol that failed appears in errors instead.

asks.description: Ask (sell-side) levels, ascending by price (best/lowest ask first). Empty when the book is unavailable.

price.description: Limit price at this level, as a decimal string.

quantity.description: Aggregate shares resting at this price level.

bids.description: Bid (buy-side) levels, descending by price (best/highest bid first). Empty when the book is unavailable.

price.description: Limit price at this level, as a decimal string.

quantity.description: Aggregate shares resting at this price level.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol this book is for.

errors.description: Per-symbol failures. A symbol appears here or in books, never both. Empty when every requested symbol succeeded.

error.description: Why this symbol's book is unavailable: it did not resolve to an equity, the market is closed, or the snapshot request failed.

symbol.description: Requested symbol that could not be returned.

guide.description: Each book in books is one symbol's snapshot: asks are sell-side levels (lowest/best ask first) and bids are buy-side levels (highest/best bid first), so the inside market is asks[0] vs bids[0]. quantity is the resting share size at a level — a level with outsized size relative to its neighbors is a wall (support on the bid side, resistance on the ask side). A requested symbol appears in books OR in errors, never both: surface anything in errors (a symbol that did not resolve to an equity, or whose book was unavailable because the market is closed) rather than implying its book was empty. An empty asks or bids array means no resting liquidity on that side right now, not an error.

tools.name: get_equity_quotes
tools.description: Get real-time stock quotes and the official last-completed-session close for one or more symbols.

symbols.description: One or more stock symbols. Above 20 symbols, quotes still return but closes is omitted with closes_error set.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

closes_error.description: Populated when the closes lookup is skipped or fails tool-wide (e.g. too many symbols, upstream timeout, no previous_close_date). Per-symbol gaps surface as a missing close on the affected result, not here.

results.description: One entry per symbol resolved from /quotes/, in upstream order, each carrying the live quote and (when available) the official prior-session close. Empty if no symbol resolved.

close.description: Official settled close from the most recent completed session. Absent when closes_error is set, or when this symbol had no close in an otherwise-successful closes batch.

date.description: YYYY-MM-DD trading date for this close. May be earlier than the requested date when interpolated.

interpolated.description: True when the requested date had no session and the price came from an earlier date.

price.description: Official settled close price. Null when the upstream has no close (rare).

symbol.description: Ticker symbol.

quote.description: Live market data for this symbol.

adjusted_previous_close.description: Prior-day close adjusted for splits, dividends, and mergers.

ask_price.description: Lowest current sell-order price. Zero when the book is closed or unavailable.

bid_price.description: Highest current buy-order price. Zero when the book is closed or unavailable.

has_traded.description: False for instruments that have never traded; price fields are meaningless when false.

last_non_reg_trade_price.description: Most recent pre-market / after-hours / overnight trade. Null during regular hours or when the symbol does not trade in the current session.

last_trade_price.description: Most recent regular-hours trade (9:30-16:00 ET). Stays frozen at the closing print after the close until the next open; not the official settled close.

previous_close.description: Raw prior closing print — not corporate-action adjusted and not the official settled close.

previous_close_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD trading date that previous_close corresponds to. Null for symbols that have never had a settled close.

state.description: Instrument listing state — 'active' is normal; other values (inactive, delisted, unlisted) mean the instrument is not in normal trading state.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol.

venue_last_non_reg_trade_time.description: Timestamp of last_non_reg_trade_price. Reveals which non-regular session the trade came from.

guide.description: Each entry in results pairs the live quote with the official prior-session close for the same symbol. Current price: pick whichever of quote.last_trade_price / quote.last_non_reg_trade_price has the more recent timestamp, then verify that timestamp is recent before calling it "current" — otherwise phrase as "as of <time>". Daily change uses quote.adjusted_previous_close. "Yesterday's close" uses results[].close.price (official settled close); when a result is missing close — either closes_error is set tool-wide, or this symbol had no close in an otherwise-successful batch — fall back to quote.previous_close and tell the user the official close lookup is unavailable. Drop bid/ask when zero. Surface has_traded=false or any non-'active' state before quoting a price.

tools.name: get_equity_tax_lots
tools.description: List the open tax lots for one equity holding in an account — each lot is a separate acquisition with its own quantity, cost basis, acquisition date, and long/short-term status. Requires a symbol (tax lots are tracked per instrument). Use it for cost-basis, holding-period, or which-lots-would-sell questions.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor. Omit for the first page; for the next page, pass the cursor query param from the prior response's next URL.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol of the holding whose tax lots you want, e.g. AAPL. Tax lots are tracked per instrument — one symbol per call.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

next.description: Next-page URL. Empty when there are no more pages.

symbol.description: Echoed symbol these tax lots belong to.

tax_lots.description: Open tax lots for this holding on this page. Empty when the cursor has no rows or the holding has no open lots.

cost_per_share.description: Tax cost basis per share (tax_cost_basis / quantity). Omitted when not yet available — e.g. a lot that hasn't finished syncing, missing cost basis, or zero quantity.

is_selectable.description: Whether this lot can be chosen in a specified-lot (lot selection) sell order. false for a lot that hasn't finished syncing yet — e.g. one acquired today (intraday) — which cannot be sold by lot selection until it does. Always present; not omitted when false, since false is the meaningful signal.

open_date.description: Acquisition date (YYYY-MM-DD) that starts the holding period. Empty when unknown.

open_lot_id.description: UUID identifying this open tax lot.

open_tran_type.description: How the lot was acquired (e.g. a buy or transfer transaction type).

order_id.description: UUID of the order that opened this lot, when it came from a Robinhood order. Absent for transfer, reward, or corporate-action lots.

quantity.description: Shares in this lot. Negative for short-position lots.

quantity_available.description: Shares in this lot not already earmarked by an open sell order (max(0, quantity - earmarked)). Use this for 'how much of this lot can I still sell?'.

tax_cost_basis.description: Total tax cost basis for the lot. Omitted when not yet available.

term.description: Holding-period classification: 'lt' (long-term, held > 1 year) or 'st' (short-term). Other values ('or', 'mixed', 'lt-or', 'st-or', 'st40lt60', 'unknown') appear for special cases. Empty when not yet classified.

guide.description: Lots are newest-acquired first by default. quantity is per-lot, not the whole position — sum quantity across lots for total shares, and quantity_available for total still-sellable shares. For which-lots-can-I-sell questions, only offer lots with is_selectable=true; a false value means the lot is still syncing (e.g. acquired today) and can't be picked for a specified-lot sell yet. Compare open_date to today to explain when a short-term lot crosses into long-term (term). When cost_per_share or tax_cost_basis is absent the basis is still pending — say so rather than treating it as zero. No live price here — for current value or unrealized gain/loss, call get_equity_quotes and combine with cost_per_share. Treat all figures as informational, not tax or performance advice. Page only when the user needs more than the first page.

tools.name: get_equity_technical_indicators
tools.description:
Compute a technical indicator (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, moving averages, ATR, VWAP, and more) over one equity symbol's OHLCV bars across a time range. For the raw OHLCV bars themselves, use get_equity_historicals.

Parameter rules:
- The parameters an indicator accepts depend on type:
  - period only: ema/sma (default 9); rsi/cci/atr/mfi (default 14); williams_r/adx (default 10); momentum (default 12); roc (default 14); donchian_channels (default 20).
  - bollinger_bands: period (default 20) + num_std (default 2).
  - macd: fast_period (12), slow_period (26), signal_period (9).
  - keltner_channels: period (default 20) + multiplier (default 2).
  - supertrend: period (default 10) + multiplier (default 3).
  - pivot_points: method (only 'classic').
  - vwap, obv: no parameters.
  Omit a parameter to use its default. Passing a parameter the chosen type does not accept is rejected.
- interval is REQUIRED — indicator periods are counted in bars, so there is no auto-selection.
- adjustment_type defaults to 'split'; 'all' (split + dividend) requires a day-or-coarser interval.
- If the requested range plus the indicator's warm-up exceeds the per-request bar cap, narrow the range or coarsen the interval.

adjustment_type.description: Corporate-action adjustment: 'none' (raw prices), 'split' (default), or 'all' (split + dividend; requires a day-or-coarser interval).

bounds.description: Session bounds. One of 'regular' (RTH, default) or 'extended'.

end_time.description: End of the range (RFC3339 UTC). Optional — defaults to the current time when omitted.

interval.description: Required bar interval the indicator is computed on. Intraday: 15second, 30second, minute, 5minute, 10minute, 30minute, hour, 4hour. Interday: day, week, month, 3month, 6month, year, 5year, 10year, 20year, 50year. The 1-minute bar is named 'minute' (not '1minute').

method.description: Calculation method. pivot_points only; currently only 'classic'.

multiplier.description: Band/offset multiplier. keltner_channels (default 2) and supertrend (default 3) only.

num_std.description: Number of standard deviations for the bands. bollinger_bands only (default 2).

output.description: How much of the series to return: 'series' (default, full range), 'latest' (most recent bar only), or 'last:N' (most recent N bars). The indicator is always computed over the full range first; this only trims the response.

period.description: Lookback period in bars. Applies to ema, sma, rsi, momentum, roc, cci, williams_r, atr, mfi, adx, donchian_channels, bollinger_bands, keltner_channels, supertrend. Omit to use the indicator's default.

start_time.description: Start of the range (RFC3339 UTC, e.g. '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z'). Required.

symbol.description: Stock symbol (uppercase). Exactly one symbol per call.

type.description: Indicator to compute. One of: ema, sma, rsi, momentum, roc, cci, williams_r, atr, mfi, adx, donchian_channels, bollinger_bands, macd, keltner_channels, supertrend, vwap, obv, pivot_points.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

bounds.description: Session bounds echoed from the request.

indicators.description: Computed indicators. Exactly one entry (one indicator per request).

params.description: Numeric parameters actually used after defaults were applied, e.g. {"period":14} or {"fast_period":12,"slow_period":26,"signal_period":9}.

series.description: Output bars in ascending time order, after the warm-up region is dropped. Trimmed per the request's output mode (full series, the latest bar, or the last N).

items.description: One bar of indicator output: 'begins_at' plus one or more numeric output fields whose names depend on the indicator type — 'value' for single-output indicators; 'upper'/'middle'/'lower' for bollinger_bands and channel indicators; 'macd'/'signal'/'histogram' for macd. A field is null when the value is undefined for that bar.

begins_at.description: Bar start timestamp (RFC3339 UTC), left-edge labeled.

interval.description: Bar interval the indicator was computed on, echoed from the request.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol echoed from the request.

guide.description: Convert begins_at (UTC) to the user's timezone for display. Skip a null output field rather than presenting it as zero. When an indicator emits multiple output fields per bar (e.g. bollinger_bands, macd), interpret them together, not in isolation.

tools.name: get_equity_tradability
tools.description: Check tradability for up to 10 equity symbols on a given account: per-session eligibility and fractional. Call before placing an order to surface restrictions. Exact-ticker match — no name or partial-ticker resolution.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts.

symbols.description: Stock symbols, max 10 per call. With more than 10, split across multiple calls of 10 or fewer. Exact-ticker match only.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

not_found.description: Symbols that did not resolve (likely delisted, invalid, or non-equity).

results.description: One entry per resolved symbol, in request order.

account_type_tradabilities.description: Per-account-type tradability for every account type the user's affiliate supports.

all_day_tradability.description: Extended/all-day session (4 AM – 8 PM ET) eligibility. 'all_day_tradability_tradable' opens extended_hours and all_day_hours.

country.description: ISO country code of the listing.

extended_hours_fractional_tradability.description: Informational; effectively always false at order-placement today.

fractional_tradability.description: Decimal-quantity eligibility; non-'tradable' values require whole-share quantities.

internal_halt_sessions.description: Sessions covered by the halt: any of 'regular_hours', 'extended_hours', 'all_day_hours'.

name.description: Full instrument name (e.g. 'Apple Inc. - Common Stock').

short_selling_tradability.description: Short-side eligibility; independent of long-side flags.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol.

tradeable.description: Listed and clearable on Robinhood; false routes nothing.

twenty_four_seven_tradability.description: Overnight 24/7 eligibility. Absent when the session is not applicable.

guide.description: Headline answer: use the account_type_tradabilities entry matching the user's account_type. If account_type_tradabilities is empty, report that account-type tradability could not be determined and rely on tradeable + state alone. Any of tradeable=false, state='inactive', or 'untradable' overrides everything below — surface first. Session mapping: regular_hours uses the headline; extended_hours / all_day_hours need all_day_tradability='all_day_tradability_tradable'; 24/7 needs twenty_four_seven_tradability='twenty_four_seven_tradability_tradable'. Mention internal_halt_* only when internal_halt_sessions overlaps the session the user wants. Fractional and dollar-based orders place only in regular_hours regardless of the flags. See trading://market-hours for session clock times.

tools.name: get_financials
tools.description: Get a company's reported financial metrics over time — revenue, gross profit, net income, and net margin — by fiscal period (annual or quarterly), for one or more symbols. Use this for fundamental analysis like revenue-growth and margin-trend tracking, profitability screens, and period-over-period comparisons. Read-only.

limit.description: Number of most-recent periods to return per symbol (e.g. 8 for the last 8 quarters or years). Defaults to 4; values above 40 are capped to 40.

period.description: Reporting period: 'quarterly' or 'annual'. Defaults to 'quarterly' when omitted.

symbols.description: One or more stock symbols (max 20 per call). Exact-ticker match — no name or partial-ticker resolution. Lowercase and whitespace-padded input is normalized to uppercase-trimmed before forwarding.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

results.description: Per-symbol financials, positionally aligned to the requested symbols (same order). A null entry means that symbol could not be resolved or has no reported financials.

financials.description: Reporting periods, most recent first, up to the requested limit. Fewer than the limit when less history is available.

fiscal_quarter.description: Fiscal quarter (1-4) for quarterly periods. Null for annual periods — use it to tell the two apart.

fiscal_year.description: Fiscal year of the reporting period (e.g. 2026).

gross_profit.description: Gross profit (revenue minus cost of goods sold) for the period, as a decimal string. Null when unavailable.

net_income.description: Bottom-line net income for the period, as a decimal string. Null when unavailable.

net_margin.description: Net margin (net income divided by revenue) for the period, expressed as a percentage (e.g. 12.34 means 12.34%). Null when unavailable.

revenue.description: Total revenue for the period, as a decimal string. Null when unavailable for the period — present as 'not available', not zero.

period.description: Echo of the reporting period for this entry: 'quarterly' or 'annual'.

symbol.description: The requested ticker, in canonical uppercase form.

guide.description: results is positional — aligned one-to-one with the requested symbols, in order. A null entry means that symbol could not be resolved (invalid, delisted, or non-equity) or has no reported financials available; surface it as "no data for X" rather than dropping it silently. Within an entry, financials is most-recent-first. Tell quarterly from annual rows by fiscal_quarter (null for annual). Present null metric values as "not available", not zero. net_margin is already a percentage — do not multiply by 100.

tools.name: get_index_quotes
tools.description: Get real-time values for one or more market indexes by instrument ID. Returns current index level, state, and timestamps.

instrument_ids.description: One or more index instrument IDs (UUIDs) to fetch current values for. Obtain IDs from the get_indexes tool.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

quotes.description: One entry per requested instrument_id with a current value. May be empty if no instrument_id resolved to an active index.

instrument_id.description: UUID identifying this index instrument. Match against the IDs returned by get_indexes.

state.description: Trading state of the index. Empty string when the upstream did not populate it; otherwise an exchange-supplied status string. Surface to the user only if non-empty and not 'active'.

symbol.description: Index ticker (e.g. SPX, NDX, DJI).

updated_at.description: When marketdata last updated the record (RFC3339). May trail venue_timestamp by a small delta.

value.description: Current index level as a decimal string (e.g. "5200.00" for the S&P 500). Quoted to preserve precision.

venue_timestamp.description: When the exchange last published this value (RFC3339).

guide.description: Match quotes[] entries to your requested instrument_ids by instrument_id (NOT symbol — multiple instruments could theoretically share a symbol). value is the current index level as a decimal string; quote precision is preserved. Surface symbol and value first; only mention state when it is non-empty and not 'active'. venue_timestamp reflects when the exchange published the level; updated_at reflects when marketdata last refreshed the record.

tools.name: get_indexes
tools.description:
Get index data for market indexes by symbol.
Optionally pass a comma-separated list of symbols (e.g. 'SPX,NDX,DJI') to filter results. Omit symbols to return all available indexes.

symbols.description: Comma-separated list of index symbols to look up (e.g. 'SPX,NDX'). Omit to return all available indexes.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

indexes.description: One entry per index in the current page. May be empty when filtering by a symbol that does not match any indexes.

current_value.description: Latest known index level as a decimal string. Snapshot only — for live values prefer get_index_quotes.

id.description: UUID identifying this index instrument. Pass to get_index_quotes to fetch the current value.

symbol.description: Index ticker (e.g. SPX, NDX, DJI).

trade_halted.description: True when trading on the index is currently halted.

updated_at.description: When this record was last updated (RFC3339).

guide.description: Each indexes[] entry identifies one index by id and symbol. current_value is a snapshot — for live values, pass id to get_index_quotes.

tools.name: get_option_chains
tools.description: List option chains for one or more underlyings. A chain describes the full set of expiration dates and contracts for a given underlying. One of underlying_symbol or ids is required.

underlying_symbol.description: Ticker filter; covers equity and index underlyings (e.g. 'AAPL', 'SPX').

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

chains.description: Option chains matching the filter. Empty when nothing resolves.

can_open_position.description: Whether new positions can be opened on this chain.

cash_component.description: Cash component for adjusted chains; null when none.

extended_hours_state.description: 'enabled' or 'disabled'. CURB market_hours values require 'enabled' AND an index chain.

id.description: Chain UUID; pass to get_option_instruments as chain_id.

late_close_state.description: Late-close state; 'enabled' adds 15 minutes of trading after the regular close.

cutoff_price.description: Price boundary between the below/above tick regimes.

sellout_time_to_expiration.description: Seconds before expiration when Robinhood begins force-closing positions on this chain. Always 0 for index options — sellout does not apply to index chains.

settle_on_open.description: True for AM-settled expirations, false for PM. When an underlying has multiple chains, they may not split cleanly by AM/PM — check per chain.

symbol.description: Chain ticker (may differ from underlying — e.g. 'SPX' for an index chain).

underlying_instruments.description: Underlying equity instruments. Empty for index chains.

symbol.description: Underlying equity ticker.

next.description: Next-page URL. Empty when there are no more pages.

guide.description: Pass id to get_option_instruments as chain_id to list contracts. CURB market_hours on place_option_order require extended_hours_state='enabled' AND an index chain (underlying_instruments empty). When the user mentions AM/PM/morning/evening expiration, check settle_on_open per chain — same underlying can have multiple chains, so do not assume they split cleanly by settlement. Present id, symbol, expiration_dates (annotate AM when settle_on_open is true), and trade_value_multiplier.

tools.name: get_option_instruments
tools.description: List option contracts. One of chain_symbol, chain_id, or ids is required; narrow further with expiration_dates, strike_price, type, state. When looking up contracts for a specific expiration, call this in parallel for every chain whose expiration_dates (from get_option_chains) includes the date. For AM/PM/morning/evening preferences, first check settle_on_open on each chain via get_option_chains and only query matching chains.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor. Omit for the first page; for the next page, pass the cursor query param from the prior response's next URL.

state.description: 'active' (default), 'expired', or 'inactive'. Use 'expired' to find option contracts whose expiration date has passed; 'inactive' is for delisted/withdrawn contracts that never expired.

tradability.description: 'tradable' or 'untradable' (untradable is rejected at the tool layer).

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

instruments.description: Option contracts matching the filter. Empty when none match.

id.description: Option instrument UUID; reference this in an order's legs.

cutoff_price.description: Price boundary between the below/above tick regimes.

sellout_datetime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which Robinhood may force-close a position in this contract. Authoritative answer — handles index/equity, AM/PM settle, late-close, and T-1 logic per contract.

tradability.description: 'tradable' or 'untradable'; active contracts may still be untradable near expiration or during halts.

underlying_type.description: 'equity' or 'index'. Matches the underlying_type filter on get_option_orders / get_option_positions.

next.description: Next-page URL. Empty when there are no more pages.

guide.description: Present chain_symbol, expiration_date, type, strike_price; flag state when not 'active' or tradability when not 'tradable'. Pass id to an order's leg.option_id. Quote sellout_datetime when the user asks "when will Robinhood force-close this position?" — it is the authoritative per-contract timestamp covering equity/index, AM/PM settle, and T-1 logic. underlying_type ('equity' or 'index') is informational; mention only when it affects strategy. When next is non-null, extract the cursor query param and call this tool again.

tools.name: get_option_level_upgrade_info
tools.description: Get the upgrade URL to apply for options access on an account. Call when option_level is null. option_level_2 enables long calls/puts, covered calls, and cash-secured puts; option_level_3 adds spreads and complex strategies, though multi-leg orders are not yet supported here.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number to generate the upgrade URL for. Obtain from get_accounts.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

account_number.description: The account this upgrade URL applies to.

upgrade_url.description: Direct link to apply for options access. Present this to the user as a tappable link.

guide.description: Send the upgrade_url to the user as a tappable link. The user must click the link, complete the full options application flow, and get approved. Once the user confirms they have been successfully approved: call get_accounts immediately with a fresh request. Do not use any option_level value from a previous get_accounts call — that data is stale until re-fetched. The option_level field on the new get_accounts response will confirm the new access level. If you do not yet have the account_number, call get_accounts first.

tools.name: get_option_orders
tools.description:
Fetch options orders for an account — list mode (newest first; open and closed, including fills, cancellations, and rejections) or single-order mode by passing order_id. When the user asks for "orders" without specifying equity or options, call both get_option_orders and get_equity_orders in parallel.

Filtering tips:
- Prefer narrow queries: combine state and/or created_at_gte for specific questions — the per-page cap is fixed.
- chain_ids filters by underlying chain UUID (from get_option_chains).
- created_at_gte: interpret relative times in the user's timezone, convert to UTC before sending.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts.

chain_ids.description: Comma-separated chain UUIDs (from get_option_chains) to filter by underlying.

created_at_gte.description: Lower bound (inclusive). ISO 8601 UTC or YYYY-MM-DD; naive values are interpreted as UTC.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor. Omit for the first page; for the next page, pass the cursor query param from the prior response's next URL.

order_id.description: Filter to a single order by UUID. The response shape is unchanged (orders[] with at most one entry); empty when the order does not belong to account_number.

state.description: Filter by single state: queued, confirmed, partially_filled, filled, rejected, cancelled, failed, voided, pending_cancelled.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

next.description: Next-page URL. Empty when there are no more pages.

orders.description: Option orders on this page, newest first. Empty when the cursor has no rows.

closing_strategy.description: Recognized closing strategy; null when not derivable.

direction.description: 'debit' (you pay) or 'credit' (you receive) on the overall order.

is_replaceable.description: True when this order is eligible for replace_option_order — cancelable, not already pending-cancel, and not in an in-flight replacement chain.

last_transaction_at.description: ISO 8601 time of the most recent fill/cancel/reject; null on a brand-new unfilled order.

legs.description: Per-leg detail; single-leg orders are standard.

quantity.description: Contracts filled in this execution.

option_id.description: Option instrument UUID. May be omitted on legacy orders that only carry the option URL.

ratio_quantity.description: Contracts for this leg per unit of the order's quantity.

opening_strategy.description: Recognized opening strategy (e.g. 'long_call', 'iron_condor'); null when not derivable.

placed_agent.description: Source that placed the order: 'user' (app), 'agentic' (MCP), 'recurring', 'drip', etc.

processed_premium.description: Premium realized on fills so far.

quantity.description: Contracts originally requested.

state.description: Lifecycle state: queued, confirmed, partially_filled, filled, rejected, cancelled, failed, voided, pending_cancelled.

stop_price.description: Stop trigger; null for non-stop orders.

type.description: Order type: 'limit' or 'market'. Combine with trigger to recover user-facing type: limit+immediate=limit, market+immediate=market, limit+stop=stop_limit, market+stop=stop_market.

guide.description: Describe orders by chain_symbol, opening_strategy or closing_strategy, state, quantity (with processed_quantity when partially filled), and premium; show id when cancellation may follow. Source chain_symbol from the response directly — never infer. Mention placed_agent when not 'user'. Recover the user-facing order type from type+trigger (limit+immediate=limit, market+immediate=market, limit+stop=stop_limit, market+stop=stop_market) and surface price for limit/stop_limit, stop_price for stop_limit/stop_market. is_replaceable=true marks orders eligible for replace_option_order (cancel-and-replace on the same contract). When next is non-null, extract the cursor query param and call this tool again.

tools.name: get_option_positions
tools.description: List options positions for an account. Returns open and closed (zero-quantity) positions. Pass nonzero=true for "what options do I have" / "show me my positions" — the common case.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor. Omit for the first page; for the next page, pass the cursor query param from the prior response's next URL.

nonzero.description: True to return only currently-open positions; omit/false to include closed ones.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

next.description: Next-page URL. Empty when there are no more pages.

positions.description: Option positions on this page. Empty when the cursor has no rows.

average_price.description: Signed cost basis per contract; negative on credit-received short positions.

intraday_average_open_price.description: Average open price across today's fills.

intraday_quantity.description: Contracts opened in the current session; resets daily.

opened_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the current open of this position began (resets on close+reopen). Prefer this over the underlying record's created_at when telling the user when they opened the position.

option_id.description: Option instrument UUID. Pass to get_option_instruments to look up strike/expiration/type.

quantity.description: Net contracts held; zero means closed.

guide.description: Surface chain_symbol, type, quantity, average_price, and expiration_date. Look up strike/option_type (call/put) via get_option_instruments with option_id when needed. Warn the user about any non-zero pending_* quantities. When next is non-null, extract the cursor query param and call this tool again.

tools.name: get_option_quotes
tools.description: Get real-time quotes for one or more option contracts by instrument UUID, plus the official prior-session close for each.

instrument_ids.description: Option instrument UUIDs. Above 20, quotes still return but closes is omitted with closes_error set.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

closes_error.description: Populated when the closes lookup is skipped or fails tool-wide (e.g. too many contracts, upstream timeout, no previous_close_date). Per-contract gaps surface as a missing close on the affected result, not here.

results.description: One entry per requested contract, in upstream quote order, each carrying the live quote and (when available) the official prior-session close. Empty if no instrument_id resolved.

close.description: Official settled close from the most recent completed session. Absent when closes_error is set, or when this contract had no close in an otherwise-successful closes batch.

date.description: YYYY-MM-DD date this close corresponds to. May be earlier than the requested date when interpolated.

instrument_id.description: UUID of the option contract; the canonical match key against quotes[].instrument_id.

interpolated.description: True when the close came from a different date than requested (illiquid contract with no print on the requested day).

price.description: Official settled close price. Null when the upstream has no close (rare; usually illiquid expired contracts).

symbol.description: Underlying ticker. NOT unique — many contracts share a symbol.

quote.description: Live market data for this option contract.

adjusted_mark_price.description: Mark adjusted for corporate actions on the underlying. Use against historical cost basis.

ask_price.description: Lowest current sell-side price. Zero when the book is closed or unavailable.

bid_price.description: Highest current buy-side price. Zero when the book is closed or unavailable.

break_even_price.description: Underlying price at which the position breaks even at expiration (strike +/- premium).

chance_of_profit_long.description: Model probability the long side ends in profit at expiration, decimal.

chance_of_profit_short.description: Model probability the short side ends in profit at expiration, decimal.

delta.description: Sensitivity of option price to a $1 move in the underlying.

gamma.description: Rate of change of delta per $1 move in the underlying.

high_fill_rate_buy_price.description: Upper bound of the likely fast-fill range for a buy.

high_fill_rate_sell_price.description: Upper bound of the likely fast-fill range for a sell.

instrument_id.description: UUID of the option contract; the canonical match key against closes[].instrument_id.

low_fill_rate_buy_price.description: Lower bound of the likely fast-fill range for a buy.

low_fill_rate_sell_price.description: Lower bound of the likely fast-fill range for a sell.

mark_price.description: Mid-market fair value — canonical current price for valuation.

open_interest.description: Outstanding contracts not yet closed or expired.

previous_close_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD trading date that previous_close_price corresponds to.
