previous_close_price.description: Quote-derived prior close — best-effort, not the official settled close.

rho.description: Sensitivity to a 1% change in interest rates.

theta.description: Daily time decay ($/day); negative for long positions.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the quote refresh.

vega.description: Sensitivity to a 1% change in implied volatility.

guide.description: Each entry in results pairs the live quote with the official prior-session close for the same option contract. Current price: use quote.mark_price (adjusted_mark_price when comparing against historical cost basis); verify quote.updated_at is recent before calling it 'current' — otherwise phrase as 'as of <updated_at>'. 1D P&L per contract: (quote.mark_price - close.price) × multiplier × position_quantity (multiplier is typically 100; use trade_value_multiplier from get_option_positions for adjusted contracts). Prefer results[].close.price (official settled close) over quote.previous_close_price; when a result is missing close — either closes_error is set tool-wide, or this contract had no close in an otherwise-successful batch — fall back to quote.previous_close_price and tell the user the official close lookup is unavailable. When close.interpolated is true, surface close.date — the price is not from the requested day. Drop bid/ask when zero.

tools.name: get_option_watchlist
tools.description: List the single-leg option contracts on the user's options watchlist. Use this instead of get_watchlist_items for the options watchlist — get_watchlist_items returns a generic shape that drops the option-specific title and the upstream rejects it with 400 anyway. Works for both equity options (AAPL, NVDA) and index options (SPX, NDX, RUT). Multi-leg strategies (verticals, condors, etc.) that may exist in the user's watchlist from app-side order placement are not shown — direct the user to the Robinhood app to view those.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

items.description: Items on the user's options watchlist. Empty when the watchlist exists but is empty.

expiration_date.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the contract's expiration.

id.description: Opaque UUID handle for this watchlist item. Same value upstream calls object_id.

name.description: Human-readable position title, e.g. "Long Call AAPL Jan 2026 $200". Surface to the user verbatim.

option_ids.description: Option contract UUIDs in this strategy. Always length 1 — only single-leg strategies are surfaced. Pass to get_option_quotes, review_option_order, place_option_order, or remove_option_from_watchlist.

list_id.description: The options watchlist this call resolved to.

guide.description: Surface name to the user — it is the human-readable position title (e.g. "Long Call AAPL Jan 2026 $200"). option_ids[0] is the contract id to pass to get_option_quotes, review_option_order, place_option_order, or remove_option_from_watchlist. If the user mentions a multi-leg strategy (vertical, iron condor, etc.) they have in their watchlist and you don't see it here, tell them this tool only shows single-leg items and to check the app. "no options watchlist found" in the error means options trading is not enabled for this account.

tools.name: get_pnl_trade_history
tools.description: Get a customer's per-trade realized profit & loss — a chronological, paginated list of closed/realizing trades (equities, options, crypto, prediction markets) with symbol, side, quantity, price, and realized gain/loss. This is the same data behind the app's PnL hub ("Realized profit & loss"). Read-only. Trades only. Use get_realized_pnl for aggregate/bucketed totals.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number (the rhs_account_number from get_accounts). Obtain it from get_accounts.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response's next_cursor. Omit for the first page.

span.description: Preset window: week (default), month, 3month, ytd, or all. Wormhole offers preset spans only (no arbitrary date range).

symbol.description: Optional single stock symbol filter (trimmed + uppercased). Omit for all symbols; one symbol per call.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

account_number.description: The account the trades are for (echoed).

next_cursor.description: Pagination cursor for the next page; empty when there are no more trades.

span.description: The span window actually used (defaults to week).

trades.description: Closed/realizing trades in the window, most-recent-first. Empty when there were none.

quantity.description: Shares/contracts, decimal string.

realized_gain.description: Realized profit/loss on this closing trade, decimal string in the account currency.

symbol.description: Instrument symbol traded.

timestamp.description: RFC3339 time the trade closed (the realizing event).

guide.description: trades are returned most-recent-first. Each entry is a closed/realizing trade with the realized gain/loss on it. price is the execution (close) price. The window is a preset span (not an arbitrary date range); default is the last week. Paginate with cursor: pass next_cursor from the previous response for the next page; an empty next_cursor means there are no more trades. An empty trades list means there were no realized trades in the window — a valid result, not an error. To filter more than one symbol, call once per symbol. Treat figures as informational, not a tax document or tax/performance advice.

tools.name: get_popular_watchlists
tools.description: Discover Robinhood-curated lists the user can follow (e.g. '100 Most Popular', 'Daily Movers'). Use to find a list_id, then pass it to follow_watchlist.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

lists.description: Robinhood-curated lists available for the user to follow.

display_name.description: Human-readable list name (e.g. '100 Most Popular', 'Daily Movers').

is_badged.description: True for newly-published curated lists. Worth surfacing as 'new' when listing options for the user to follow.

item_count.description: Number of items in the list.

next.description: Cursor URL for the next page when more results exist. Absent on the last page.

guide.description: For 'this list is new' surfacing, use is_badged. The response is paginated — if next is non-null there are more lists; call again with the cursor from next.

tools.name: get_portfolio
tools.description: Get the account's portfolio market value breakdown by asset type and buying power. Use for "how much is my account worth?", "what's my portfolio breakdown?", "how much do I have in options?", and "how much can I spend / afford?" questions.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Obtain from get_accounts.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

buying_power.description: Real-time buying power from the broker.

Authoritative spendable figure used to gate order placement. For margin-enabled accounts it already includes available margin leverage; for cash and leverage-disabled accounts it equals the cash-only figure.

intraday_buying_power.description: Buying power for intraday leverage, when applicable; null when the account does not have intraday margin enabled.

off_intraday_buying_power.description: Buying power outside the intraday-leverage window; null when not applicable.

unleveraged_buying_power.description: Buying power ignoring margin leverage. Typically <= buying_power. Negative when the account is in deficit — new positions cannot be opened until the deficit is cleared.

cash.description: Available cash (includes early access from pending deposits).

crypto_buying_power.description: Spendable buying power for crypto buys. Cash-only (crypto is not marginable), so it excludes the equity margin leverage that buying_power may include — quote this for 'how much can I spend on crypto'. Omitted when unavailable or when the caller is not in the crypto experiment.

buying_power.description: Authoritative spendable figure for crypto buys (cash-only; excludes equity margin leverage).

currency.description: Currency all values are denominated in (e.g. USD).

equity_value.description: Value of stock and ETF positions.

futures_value.description: Value of futures positions (includes RHD cost basis).

pending_deposits.description: Deposits in transit, not yet settled.

total_value.description: Total portfolio value across all asset types. The default 'account value' to show the user.

guide.description: Show total_value as "account value" or "portfolio value". The breakdown fields show how value is distributed across asset types. Zero-value fields mean the user has no holdings in that asset class — omit them from display unless the user explicitly asks. For "how much cash do I have?" use the cash field. buying_power is the real-time authoritative spendable figure from the broker; quote buying_power.buying_power as the primary affordability answer with buying_power.display_currency. For "how much can I spend on crypto?" quote crypto_buying_power.buying_power instead — crypto is cash-only (not marginable), so the top-level buying_power may overstate it; crypto_buying_power is omitted when unavailable. crypto_value reflects the user's crypto holdings value.

tools.name: get_realized_pnl
tools.description: Get a customer's realized profit & loss for an account over a time window — per-bucket realized gain ($ and %) and the number of closing trades, plus window totals. Read-only. Aggregate, bucketed numbers only (not individual trades). Use for post-trade analysis like "how did my last 90 days of trades do?".

account_number.description: Brokerage account number (the rhs_account_number from get_accounts). Obtain it from get_accounts.

asset_classes.description: Filter to one or more of equity, option, crypto. Omit for all asset classes available on the account.

display_currency.description: Currency for returned amounts. Currently USD only; defaults to USD.

end_date.description: Custom window end, YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive — the entire end_date is covered (through 23:59:59 in timezone). Use with start_date instead of span; an end_date beyond today returns data through the present.

span.description: Preset window: day, week, month, 3month, year, or all. Defaults to 3month ('last 90 days'). Mutually exclusive with start_date/end_date.

start_date.description: Custom window start, YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive — interpreted at midnight in timezone (default US Eastern). Use with end_date instead of span; must be on or before end_date and not in the future.

timezone.description: IANA timezone for bucket day-boundaries (e.g. America/New_York). Defaults to the account timezone (US Eastern).

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

account_number.description: The account the realized profit and loss is for (echoed).

data_points.description: Realized-profit-and-loss buckets in ascending time order. Empty when there were no closing trades in the window.

end_time.description: RFC3339 end of the bucket window.

number_of_trades.description: Count of closing/realizing trades in the bucket.

rate_of_realized_gain.description: Bucket realized return as a percentage, decimal string (e.g. '10' = 10%). Null for transfer-only buckets with no cost basis — the same condition that nulls realized_gain; present as 'n/a', not 0.

realized_gain.description: Realized profit/loss in the bucket, decimal string in display_currency. Null when the bucket's realized profit and loss is not meaningful (e.g. all lots are transfers with no cost basis) — present it as 'n/a', not 0.

start_time.description: RFC3339 start of the bucket window (inclusive).

display_currency.description: Currency the amounts are denominated in.

total_rate_of_return.description: Total realized return across the window as a percentage, decimal string.

total_returns.description: Total realized profit and loss across the window, decimal string in display_currency. '0' when the window had no closing trades.

window.description: The window actually used — the span name, or 'start_date..end_date' for a custom range.

guide.description: This is realized profit & loss only — gains/losses from positions closed within the window; it excludes unrealized profit and loss on open positions. data_points are buckets in ascending time order. A window with no closing trades returns empty data_points with zero totals — that is a valid result ("no realized trades in this window"), not an error. When a bucket's realized_gain and rate_of_realized_gain are null (a transfer-only bucket whose lots had no cost basis, e.g. transfers), present them as "n/a" — do not render them as $0 / 0%. These are aggregate numbers; for individual trade records use the separate realized profit and loss (trade history) tool. Treat figures as informational, not a tax document or tax/performance advice.

tools.name: get_scanner_filter_specs
tools.description:
List every valid scanner filter type and how to use it. Call this before constructing filters for create_scan or update_scan_filters — do not guess filter_type names.

This tool takes no parameters.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

filter_specs.description: Every valid scanner filter type: wire-format enum name, category, value format, and supported predicates/intervals/lengths/plots.

display_name.description: Human-readable name as shown in Legend, e.g. "RSI".

filter_group.description: Category bucket: POPULAR, TECHNICAL, FUNDAMENTAL, OPTION, or PRICE_VOLUME.

filter_type.description: Wire-format enum name, e.g. "FILTER_TYPE_RSI". Pass this in filter payloads.

supported_intervals.description: Time granularities the filter accepts (e.g. ["1m", "5m", "1h", "1d"]). Empty when the filter has no interval input.

supported_lengths.description: Lookback lengths the filter accepts (e.g. [9, 14, 21, 50] for RSI). Empty when the filter has no length input.

supported_plots.description: Plot / price-field values the filter accepts (e.g. ["open", "close"] for % Change). Empty when the filter has no plot input.

supported_predicates.description: Symbolic predicates valid for this filter (e.g. ">", "<", "BETWEEN").

unit_type.description: Value unit: DOLLAR, PERCENTAGE, or PLAIN.

value_type.description: Expected value format: DECIMAL, INTEGER, DATETIME, or STRING.

guide.description: Match filter_type exactly, including suffixes (e.g. percent-change filters use FILTER_TYPE_PERCENT_CHANGE_FROM_CLOSE, not FILTER_TYPE_PERCENT_CHANGE). If a filter's supported_intervals is non-empty, pass an interval — omitting it is the leading cause of an otherwise-valid filter returning no results. Use supported_predicates symbols exactly (e.g. ">", "BETWEEN").

tools.name: get_scans
tools.description:
List the authenticated user's saved scanners (also called screeners). A scan is a saved set of filters and columns that filters the market for instruments matching specific criteria (e.g. "RSI > 70 and Volume > 1M"). The user creates these in Legend or via the create_scan tool.

Returns one entry per scan with its id, title, active filters, column count, sort order, and a flag indicating whether the scan is managed by Cortex (Legend's AI agent). Cortex-managed scans are read-only via MCP — they can be run with run_scan but not modified with update_scan_filters or update_scan_config.

This tool takes no parameters.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

scans.description: List of the user's saved scans, newest first. Empty when the user has no saved scans.

column_count.description: Number of visible columns configured on this scan.

cortex_managed.description: True when this scan was created or modified by Cortex (Legend's AI agent). Cortex-managed scans can be read but not modified via MCP tools.

filter_summary.description: Active filters applied to this scan, one entry per filter. Empty when the scan uses only preset defaults.

filter_type.description: Filter category in human form (e.g. "RSI", "Volume", "Market Cap"). See the scanner-filter-specs resource for the full list.

interval.description: Time granularity for time-series filters (e.g. "1d", "1h"). Empty for snapshot filters.

length.description: Lookback length for filters that need one (e.g. RSI period of 14). Zero/omitted for filters that don't take a length.

plot.description: Plot / price-field input for filters that have one (e.g. % Change uses "open" or "close" to choose changeFromOpen vs changeFromClose). Empty for filters that don't take a plot.

values.description: Threshold values for the predicate. Single-element for >/</=, two-element for BETWEEN, multi-element for IN_LIST/ANY_OF.

scan_id.description: Unique scan identifier. Pass this to run_scan, update_scan_filters, or update_scan_config.

sorting.description: Current sort column + direction in human form (e.g. "Volume desc"). Empty when no sort is configured.

title.description: Human-readable name the user gave the scan.

guide.description:
How to interpret the response:
- scans is an array, newest first. Empty when the user has no saved scans — offer to create one with create_scan if they ask.
- filter_summary on each scan is a human-readable list of the active filters: filter_type (e.g. "RSI"), predicate (>, <, BETWEEN), values, and optional interval/length. Use these to describe what the scan does without quoting raw configuration internals.
- cortex_managed: true means the scan was created or modified by Cortex inside Legend. Tell the user it's read-only via MCP if they ask to modify it. Suggest creating a fresh scan via create_scan as the alternative.
- column_count is the number of visible columns in the scan's table. Mention it only if the user asks "how wide" or about specific columns.
- sorting describes the scan's current sort (e.g. "Volume desc"). Mention it when summarizing how results would be ordered.
- Use scan_id (not title) when calling run_scan, update_scan_filters, or update_scan_config.

When presenting scans to the user:
- Render as a numbered list with the title prominent and a short summary of filters.
- Mark Cortex-managed scans clearly so the user knows they're read-only here.
- If the user wants to see results, ask which scan and call run_scan with that scan_id.

tools.name: get_watchlist_items
tools.description: List the items in a watchlist. Items may be stocks/ETFs, crypto pairs, futures, indexes — distinguished by object_type. For the options watchlist, use get_option_watchlist instead — this tool returns a generic shape that drops the strategy-specific fields and the upstream rejects it with 400 anyway. Does not return live prices; call get_quotes with the symbol(s) for that.

list_id.description: UUID of the watchlist whose items to fetch. Obtain from get_watchlists or get_popular_watchlists.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

has_futures_contracts.description: True when at least one item is a futures contract. Mention to the user when surfacing items so they know which rows are futures.

object_id.description: UUID of the underlying instrument / currency pair / futures contract / index. Pass to remove_from_watchlist (via symbol — the tool resolves it back to this id internally) to remove this item.

object_type.description: What kind of asset this item references: 'instrument' (US stock/ETF), 'currency_pair' (crypto), 'futures' (futures contract), 'index' (market index), 'option_strategy' (options watchlist only), 'tokenized_stock'. Different object types are surfaced differently in the app.

symbol.description: Human-readable ticker (e.g. 'AAPL' for instrument, 'BTC-USD' for currency_pair). May be empty for futures items if the upstream lookup failed.

guide.description: For instruments (object_type=instrument) the symbol is the ticker — pass to get_quotes for prices. For crypto (currency_pair), symbol is the pair (BTC-USD). The has_futures_contracts flag at the top tells you whether any futures items are present — futures behavior is different (the object_id is the active contract, not the product). When presenting to the user, lead with symbol + object_type; omit object_id unless asked.

tools.name: get_watchlists
tools.description: List the user's watchlists, including both user-created custom lists and Robinhood-curated lists the user follows. Use to look up list_id values for other watchlist tools.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

watchlists.description: One entry per watchlist the user has created or follows.

allowed_object_types.description: Which kinds of objects can be added to this list. Common values: instrument (US stock/ETF), currency_pair (crypto), index (market index), option_strategy (options watchlist — see add_option_to_watchlist), futures. add_to_watchlist supports instrument, currency_pair, and index; options use the dedicated add_option_to_watchlist tool; futures still require the Robinhood app.

display_description.description: Optional short description shown under the name.

icon_emoji.description: Emoji shown alongside the name. Present on custom lists; absent on Robinhood-curated lists.

id.description: List UUID. Pass to other watchlist tools (get_watchlist_items, add/remove_from_watchlist, update_watchlist) as list_id.

item_count.description: Number of items in the list.

owner_type.description: 'custom' means a user-created list. 'robinhood' means a curated list the user is following. Use this to distinguish user-owned lists (writable: rename, delete items, add items) from curated lists (read + follow/unfollow only).

guide.description: Distinguish lists by owner_type: 'custom' lists are writable (add_to_watchlist, remove_from_watchlist, update_watchlist); 'robinhood' lists are read-only and managed via follow_watchlist / unfollow_watchlist. When the user picks a list to act on, prefer matching by display_name and confirm before destructive actions.

tools.name: place_equity_order
tools.description:
Place a real equity order with real money. Parameters mirror review_equity_order plus the optional ref_id. Requires an agentic_allowed=true account; non-agentic accounts are rejected — do not call.

Workflow: by default call review_equity_order first, present the estimated cost and any alerts, and get explicit user confirmation before calling this tool. Skip review only when the user has very explicitly asked to bypass it (e.g. "skip the review", "just place it, don't review") — a generic "place this order" is NOT a bypass.

Idempotency: pass a fresh UUID as ref_id on the first call for each logical order, and re-send the SAME ref_id on retries of transient transport failures. Use a new ref_id only when the user wants a new order.

Parameter rules:
- If the user has not specified type, ask. For immediate fills with price protection, prefer a marketable limit at the current ask over a plain market.
- Outside regular hours, only limit orders execute. For an immediate fill during extended or overnight/24-hour sessions, place a limit order (a marketable limit at the current ask) with market_hours set to that session — not a market order. Market and stop orders are regular_hours-only; placed after hours as regular_hours they queue for the next regular open.
- Provide exactly one of quantity or dollar_amount; dollar_amount requires type=market (server computes shares from last_trade_price).
- Fractional shares: only on type=market with market_hours=regular_hours, eligible accounts, up to 6 decimal places, no short sells.
- limit_price required for limit/stop_limit; stop_price required for stop_market/stop_limit.
- Fractional and dollar-based orders only place in regular_hours; the tool rejects them in other sessions.
- tax_lots (specified-lot selling, sell only): to sell specific lots, first call get_equity_tax_lots for the symbol, then pass tax_lots as {open_lot_id, quantity} pairs whose quantities sum to the order quantity. Omit for default (FIFO) cost basis. US accounts only; not allowed with dollar_amount, stop orders, all_day_hours, or fractional limit orders.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts. Must be agentic_allowed=true; non-agentic accounts are rejected.

dollar_amount.description: USD notional (e.g. '100.00'). Only valid with type=market.

limit_price.description: Limit price; required for limit or stop_limit.

market_hours.description: 'regular_hours' (default, 9:30–16:00 ET), 'extended_hours' (pre-/post-market), or 'all_day_hours' (the 24 Hour Market / overnight session). extended_hours and all_day_hours execute limit orders only — market, stop_market, and stop_limit are regular_hours-only and are rejected if tagged to another session.

quantity.description: Number of shares. Decimals (fractional) allowed for market + regular_hours only.

ref_id.description: Idempotency key (UUID). Generate once per logical order and re-send on retry — the upstream deduplicates by ref_id. Omitting falls back to a server-generated key (loses client↔gateway idempotency).

stop_price.description: Stop trigger price; required for stop_market or stop_limit.

symbol.description: Stock symbol.

tax_lots.description: Optional specified-lot selection for a SELL order. To sell specific tax lots instead of the default FIFO cost basis, pass the exact lots as {open_lot_id, quantity} objects, where open_lot_id comes from get_equity_tax_lots and the quantities sum to the order quantity. Omit for default FIFO. Sell only; at most 30 lots; US accounts only. Not allowed with dollar_amount, stop_market/stop_limit, all_day_hours, or fractional-share limit orders.

open_lot_id.description: open_lot_id of the open tax lot to sell, from get_equity_tax_lots.

quantity.description: Shares to sell from this lot as a decimal string; at most the lot's quantity_available.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

order.description: Newly-created equity order. Same shape as get_equity_orders orders[].

average_price.description: Weighted average fill price across executions. Null until the first fill.

cumulative_quantity.description: Shares filled so far. Less than quantity while partially filled; equal once filled.

dollar_based_amount.description: Notional amount for dollar-based market orders; null for share-quantity orders.

currency_code.description: ISO currency code; always 'USD' for equity orders today.

executions.description: Per-fill detail; reference only for per-lot questions.

quantity.description: Shares filled in this execution.

instrument_id.description: Instrument UUID; resolve to symbol via the instruments endpoint if needed.

last_transaction_at.description: Time of the most recent fill, cancel, or reject. Null on a brand-new unfilled order.

placed_agent.description: Source that placed the order: 'user' (app), 'agentic' (MCP), 'recurring', 'drip', 'brokeback' (system), or other internal values.

price.description: Limit price for limit/stop_limit orders; null for market orders.

quantity.description: Originally-requested share quantity. Null for dollar-based orders before the first fill.

state.description: Lifecycle state: new, queued, unconfirmed, partially_filled, filled, cancelled, rejected, failed, voided, pending_cancelled, partially_filled_rest_cancelled, and (for short-sale orders) locating, locate_failed.

stop_price.description: Stop trigger price for stop_market/stop_limit orders; null otherwise.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol.

type.description: Upstream type: 'market' or 'limit'. Combine with trigger to recover the user-facing market/limit/stop_market/stop_limit.

guide.description: The order has been submitted — not necessarily filled. Describe with state. Tell the user the order was placed and summarize symbol, side, type, quantity (or dollar_based_amount), and limit/stop prices; remind them it may not have filled yet and that they can check status or cancel via id. On error, the order was NOT placed — report the error verbatim.

tools.name: place_option_order
tools.description:
Place a real options order with real money.

Workflow: by default call review_option_order first, present the alerts, fees, collateral, and quote, and get explicit user confirmation before calling this tool. Skip review only when the user has very explicitly asked to bypass it (e.g. "skip the review", "just place it, don't review") — a generic "place this order" is NOT a bypass.

Capability: single-leg options orders only — Level 2 strategies (covered calls, cash-secured puts, long calls and puts). Multi-leg spreads (Level 3 strategies) aren't supported yet, even on option_level_3 accounts.

Account requirements: confirm via get_accounts that the chosen account is agentic_allowed=true AND has option_level_2 or option_level_3. If agentic_allowed=false do NOT call. If option_level is empty or option_level_0, do NOT call; follow the get_accounts guide for how to direct the user to enroll.

Alert handling: pre-trade alerts surface only in review_option_order. After the user has acknowledged the reviewed alert, call this tool with the same parameters.

Idempotency: pass a fresh UUID as ref_id on the first call for each logical order, and re-send the SAME ref_id on retries of transient transport failures. Use a new ref_id only when the user wants a new order.

To find option_id: get_option_chains → get_option_instruments filtered by expiration_date/strike_price/type.

Parameter rules:
- legs: exactly one leg. Direction derived from side (buy → debit, sell → credit).
- type: 'limit' (default), 'market', 'stop_limit', 'stop_market'. price for limit/stop_limit; stop_price for stop_market/stop_limit.
- market and stop_market: regular_hours + GFD only; stop_market is sell-to-close only with stop_price below the current ask.

Supported in the Robinhood apps only (not via this MCP):
- multi-leg structures: spreads, condors, butterflies, etc.

Not currently supported anywhere (including the Robinhood apps):
- combo orders. At Robinhood, "combo" specifically means a stock-option combo: one option leg paired with 100 shares of the underlying equity. In broader industry use, "combo" without qualifier sometimes refers to multi-leg option structures.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts. Must be agentic_allowed=true.

position_effect.description: 'open' (new position) or 'close' (existing position). To close a long use sell; to close a short use buy.

ratio_quantity.description: Contracts for this leg per unit of the order's quantity. Defaults to 1.

market_hours.description: 'regular_hours' (default), 'regular_curb_hours', or 'regular_curb_overnight_hours'. Non-limit-immediate orders only place in regular_hours. CURB requires an index chain with extended_hours_state='enabled'.

price.description: Limit price per contract. Required for limit/stop_limit; must be omitted for market/stop_market.

quantity.description: Positive integer contract count.

ref_id.description: Idempotency key (UUID). Generate once per logical order and re-send on retry. Omitting falls back to a server-generated key.

stop_price.description: Stop trigger price per contract. Required for stop_limit/stop_market; must be omitted for limit/market.

type.description: 'limit' (default), 'market', 'stop_limit', or 'stop_market'. Should match the type the user reviewed.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

order.description: Newly-created order. Same shape as get_option_orders orders[].

closing_strategy.description: Recognized closing strategy; null when not derivable.

direction.description: 'debit' (you pay) or 'credit' (you receive) on the overall order.

is_replaceable.description: True when this order is eligible for replace_option_order — cancelable, not already pending-cancel, and not in an in-flight replacement chain.

last_transaction_at.description: ISO 8601 time of the most recent fill/cancel/reject; null on a brand-new unfilled order.

legs.description: Per-leg detail; single-leg orders are standard.

quantity.description: Contracts filled in this execution.

option_id.description: Option instrument UUID. May be omitted on legacy orders that only carry the option URL.

ratio_quantity.description: Contracts for this leg per unit of the order's quantity.

opening_strategy.description: Recognized opening strategy (e.g. 'long_call', 'iron_condor'); null when not derivable.

placed_agent.description: Source that placed the order: 'user' (app), 'agentic' (MCP), 'recurring', 'drip', etc.

processed_premium.description: Premium realized on fills so far.

quantity.description: Contracts originally requested.

state.description: Lifecycle state: queued, confirmed, partially_filled, filled, rejected, cancelled, failed, voided, pending_cancelled.

stop_price.description: Stop trigger; null for non-stop orders.

type.description: Order type: 'limit' or 'market'. Combine with trigger to recover user-facing type: limit+immediate=limit, market+immediate=market, limit+stop=stop_limit, market+stop=stop_market.

guide.description: The order has been submitted — not necessarily filled. Describe with state. Summarize chain_symbol, opening_strategy or closing_strategy, quantity, price, direction; remind the user it may not have filled and that they can check status or cancel via id. Source chain_symbol from the response directly. On error, the order was NOT placed; report the error verbatim. CURB-rejection errors mean market_hours is incompatible with the chain or the CURB session is closed — suggest 'regular_hours' or waiting for the session.

tools.name: remove_from_watchlist
tools.description: Remove items from a watchlist. Exactly one of symbols (stocks/ETFs), currency_pair_ids (crypto), or index_ids (market indexes) is required — mutually exclusive. For options use remove_option_from_watchlist. Items not on the list are no-ops (not errors). Confirm with the user before calling.

currency_pair_ids.description: Currency-pair UUIDs to remove. Mutually exclusive with symbols and index_ids.

index_ids.description: Index UUIDs to remove. Mutually exclusive with symbols and currency_pair_ids.

list_id.description: UUID of the watchlist to remove items from.

symbols.description: Stock symbols to remove (e.g. ['AAPL']). Mutually exclusive with currency_pair_ids and index_ids.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

currency_pair_ids.description: The currency-pair UUIDs this call targeted (crypto path).

index_ids.description: The index UUIDs this call targeted (index path).

object_type.description: 'instrument' when symbols were used, 'currency_pair' when currency_pair_ids were used, 'index' when index_ids were used.

status.description: 'ok' when the upstream accepted the operation.

symbols.description: The stock symbols this call targeted (instrument path).

guide.description: On success the response echoes the operations applied. 404 means the list_id is not writable by the user.

tools.name: remove_option_from_watchlist
tools.description: Remove option contracts from the user's options watchlist. Specify the same position_type used when the contract was added (defaults to "long"). Contracts not on the list are no-ops. Confirm with the user before calling.

option_ids.description: Option contract UUIDs to remove. The position_type must match how each contract was added (most likely "long").

position_type.description: "long" (default) or "short". Must match how the contract was originally added.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

list_id.description: The options watchlist this call resolved to and targeted.

option_ids.description: Option contract UUIDs this call targeted, echoed for the caller's reference.

position_type.description: The position_type applied to every contract in this call.

status.description: "ok" when the upstream accepted the operation.

guide.description: Each option_id is matched as a single-leg position with the supplied position_type. The upstream resolves the leg back to the existing strategy and removes it. "no options watchlist found" means the user has not enabled options trading.

tools.name: review_equity_order
tools.description:
Simulate a stock order without placing it. Returns the current quote plus pre-trade alerts (buying power, PDT, instrument halt, etc.). Call this by default before place_equity_order unless the user has very explicitly asked to skip review. Requires an agentic_allowed=true account; non-agentic accounts are rejected — do not call.

Parameter rules:
- If the user has not specified type, ask. For immediate fills with price protection, prefer a marketable limit at the current ask over a plain market.
- Outside regular hours, only limit orders execute. For an immediate fill during extended or overnight/24-hour sessions, place a limit order (a marketable limit at the current ask) with market_hours set to that session — not a market order. Market and stop orders are regular_hours-only; placed after hours as regular_hours they queue for the next regular open.
- Provide exactly one of quantity or dollar_amount; dollar_amount requires type=market (server computes shares from last_trade_price).
- Fractional shares: only on type=market with market_hours=regular_hours, eligible accounts, up to 6 decimal places, no short sells.
- limit_price required for limit/stop_limit; stop_price required for stop_market/stop_limit.
- Fractional and dollar-based orders only place in regular_hours; the tool rejects them in other sessions.
- tax_lots (specified-lot selling, sell only): to sell specific lots, first call get_equity_tax_lots for the symbol, then pass tax_lots as {open_lot_id, quantity} pairs whose quantities sum to the order quantity. Omit for default (FIFO) cost basis. US accounts only; not allowed with dollar_amount, stop orders, all_day_hours, or fractional limit orders.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts. Must be agentic_allowed=true; non-agentic accounts are rejected.

dollar_amount.description: USD notional (e.g. '100.00'). Only valid with type=market.

limit_price.description: Limit price; required for limit or stop_limit.

market_hours.description: 'regular_hours' (default, 9:30–16:00 ET), 'extended_hours' (pre-/post-market), or 'all_day_hours' (the 24 Hour Market / overnight session). extended_hours and all_day_hours execute limit orders only — market, stop_market, and stop_limit are regular_hours-only and are rejected if tagged to another session.

quantity.description: Number of shares. Decimals (fractional) allowed for market + regular_hours only.

stop_price.description: Stop trigger price; required for stop_market or stop_limit.

symbol.description: Stock symbol.

tax_lots.description: Optional specified-lot selection for a SELL order. To sell specific tax lots instead of the default FIFO cost basis, pass the exact lots as {open_lot_id, quantity} objects, where open_lot_id comes from get_equity_tax_lots and the quantities sum to the order quantity. Omit for default FIFO. Sell only; at most 30 lots; US accounts only. Not allowed with dollar_amount, stop_market/stop_limit, all_day_hours, or fractional-share limit orders.

open_lot_id.description: open_lot_id of the open tax lot to sell, from get_equity_tax_lots.

quantity.description: Shares to sell from this lot as a decimal string; at most the lot's quantity_available.

time_in_force.description: 'gfd' (good for day) or 'gtc' (good till cancelled). Default: gfd.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

dollar_amount.description: Echoed dollar amount (USD). Omitted for share-quantity orders.

market_data_disclosure.description: Compliance market-data disclosure string (NBBO bid/ask with sizes + venue codes, last trade, and update time), e.g. 'Bid $218.00 × 1400 P · Ask $218.09 × 100 M · Last $218.05 × 100. Updated 1:21 PM ET.'. The update time is rendered in the user's timezone (from the x-timezone-id header, default ET). Display this verbatim to the user. Empty only when live prices were unavailable.

order_checks.description: Pre-trade alert. Empty object {} means no alerts. Non-empty shape: {alert_type: <UPPER_SNAKE_CASE>, <alert_type_lowercase>_alert_details: {...}}. Present alert_type verbatim along with the details — do not enumerate alert types; new ones ship regularly.

quantity.description: Echoed share quantity. Omitted for dollar-based orders.

quote_data.description: Live market data (single quote); same shape and semantics as get_equity_quotes results[0].quote.

adjusted_previous_close.description: Prior-day close adjusted for splits, dividends, and mergers.

ask_price.description: Lowest current sell-order price. Zero when the book is closed or unavailable.

bid_price.description: Highest current buy-order price. Zero when the book is closed or unavailable.

has_traded.description: False for instruments that have never traded; price fields are meaningless when false.

last_non_reg_trade_price.description: Most recent pre-market / after-hours / overnight trade. Null during regular hours or when the symbol does not trade in the current session.

last_trade_price.description: Most recent regular-hours trade (9:30-16:00 ET). Stays frozen at the closing print after the close until the next open; not the official settled close.

previous_close.description: Raw prior closing print — not corporate-action adjusted and not the official settled close.

previous_close_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD trading date that previous_close corresponds to. Null for symbols that have never had a settled close.

state.description: Instrument listing state — 'active' is normal; other values (inactive, delisted, unlisted) mean the instrument is not in normal trading state.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol.

venue_last_non_reg_trade_time.description: Timestamp of last_non_reg_trade_price. Reveals which non-regular session the trade came from.

symbol.description: Echoed symbol.

guide.description: This tool does NOT place the order — it returns a preview for the user to review. Reaching this tool means the user opted into the review step, so you MUST present the preview to the user (symbol, side, type, quantity or dollar_amount, limit/stop prices, estimated execution) and get explicit confirmation before calling place_equity_order. This holds even when order_checks is empty ({}) — empty means no broker alerts, NOT that confirmation can be skipped; the user asked to review, so let them review. Surface any non-empty alert in order_checks verbatim alongside the preview. In addition, you MUST always show the quotes to the user for compliance reasons: whenever market_data_disclosure is present, display it to the user exactly as provided — verbatim and unmodified — as the required compliance quote disclosure. Market orders: estimate execution from quote_data.last_trade_price (ask for buys, bid for sells when spreads are wide). Limit orders: compare limit_price to quote_data bid/ask so the user knows the distance from market.

tools.name: review_option_order
tools.description:
Simulate an options order without placing it. Returns the current quote plus pre-trade alerts. Call this by default before place_option_order or replace_option_order unless the user has very explicitly asked to skip review.

Capability: single-leg options orders only — Level 2 strategies (covered calls, cash-secured puts, long calls and puts). Multi-leg spreads (Level 3 strategies) aren't supported yet, even on option_level_3 accounts.

Workflow: confirm via get_accounts that the chosen account is agentic_allowed=true AND has option_level_2 or option_level_3. If agentic_allowed=false do NOT call. If option_level is empty or option_level_0, do NOT call — follow the get_accounts guide for how to direct the user to enroll.

Parameter rules:
- legs: exactly one leg with option_id (from get_option_instruments), side, position_effect, optional ratio_quantity.
- type: 'limit' (default), 'market', 'stop_limit', 'stop_market'. If unspecified, ask. price for limit/stop_limit; stop_price for stop_market/stop_limit.
- market and stop_market: single-leg, GFD, regular_hours only. stop_market is sell-to-close only with stop_price below the current ask. Non-limit-immediate types are blocked in any extended-hours session.
- Surface order_checks alerts verbatim — the detail strings carry the actual time/contract/BP values for the user.

account_number.description: Brokerage account number. Must come from the user or be clearly implied — never default from get_accounts. Must be agentic_allowed=true.

chain_symbol.description: Underlying ticker (e.g. 'AAPL', 'SPXW'). Supply alongside underlying_type to include fees and collateral in the response — always do so when known.

position_effect.description: 'open' (new position) or 'close' (existing position). To close a long use sell; to close a short use buy.

ratio_quantity.description: Contracts for this leg per unit of the order's quantity. Defaults to 1.

market_hours.description: 'regular_hours' (default), 'regular_curb_hours', or 'regular_curb_overnight_hours'. Extended-hours sessions only accept limit+immediate. CURB requires an index chain with extended_hours_state='enabled' (per get_option_chains); rejection surfaces at place time.

price.description: Limit price per contract (e.g. '1.50'). Required for limit/stop_limit; must be omitted for market/stop_market.

quantity.description: Positive integer contract count.

stop_price.description: Stop trigger price per contract. Required for stop_limit/stop_market; must be omitted for limit/market. For sell-side stop_market, must be below the current ask.

underlying_type.description: 'equity' or 'index'. Required alongside chain_symbol to enable the fee + collateral fetch.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

account_number.description: Echoed account_number. Mask all but the last 4 digits (e.g. '••••1234') when displaying to the user.

collateral.description: Collateral to be held if the order fills. Present only when chain_symbol and underlying_type were supplied. Warn the user when cash.infinite=true (unlimited-risk position).

account_number.description: Account the collateral applies to; echoes the request. Mask all but the last 4 digits (e.g. '••••1234') when displaying to the user.

cash.description: Dollar collateral reserved when the order fills.

amount.description: Dollar collateral reserved as a decimal string. null when the position carries infinite risk (infinite=true).

direction.description: 'debit' means buying power is held to cover the position; 'credit' means collateral is released.

infinite.description: True for naked short positions with unlimited risk (e.g. naked calls). Warn the user before they proceed.

equities.description: Share-based collateral required by the strategy (e.g. covered calls). Empty for most orders — only short equity-covered strategies require share collateral.

direction.description: 'debit' means shares are held; 'credit' means shares are released.

instrument.description: URL of the equity instrument. Prefer instrument_id (the UUID) when calling other tools; the URL is retained for clients that consume hyperlinks.

instrument_id.description: UUID of the underlying equity. Pass to get_equity_quotes / get_equity_tradability when the agent needs live data on the held equity.

quantity.description: Number of shares being held.

symbol.description: Equity symbol whose shares are being held as collateral (e.g. 'AAPL').

uncovered_shares.description: Shares of this symbol that do NOT yet cover the option position. Non-zero means additional cover is required.

fees.description: Trading fees breakdown. Present only when chain_symbol and underlying_type were supplied. Lead with total_fee when presenting.

fee.description: Dollar amount charged for this component, as a decimal string.

fee_rate.description: Rate applied to compute this fee component, as a decimal string.

fee.description: Dollar amount charged for this component, as a decimal string.

fee_rate.description: Rate applied to compute this fee component, as a decimal string.

fee.description: Dollar amount charged for this component, as a decimal string.

fee_rate.description: Rate applied to compute this fee component, as a decimal string.

gold_fee_savings.description: Gold-member fee discount applied to this order. fee_total_savings is zero for non-Gold accounts.

fee_saving_rate.description: Rate of the Gold fee discount applied to this order.

fee_total_savings.description: Total dollar savings from Gold pricing on this order. Mention to the user when non-zero.

is_gold.description: True when Gold pricing was applied to this calculation.

fee.description: Dollar amount charged for this component, as a decimal string.

fee_rate.description: Rate applied to compute this fee component, as a decimal string.

fee.description: Dollar amount charged for this component, as a decimal string.

fee_rate.description: Rate applied to compute this fee component, as a decimal string.

sales_taxes.description: Applicable sales taxes. Empty for most US accounts; surface non-empty entries separately from fee components.

display_name.description: User-facing label for this tax (e.g. 'GST'). Use this when surfacing taxes to the user.

fee.description: Dollar amount of this sales tax line as a decimal string.

fee_rate.description: Rate applied to compute this tax.

total_fee.description: All-in fee the user will pay (or save, if negative due to Gold savings). Lead with this when presenting fees.

legs.description: Echoed request legs[] (option_id, side, position_effect, ratio_quantity per leg) so the response identifies which contract(s) were reviewed.

position_effect.description: 'open' (new position) or 'close' (existing position). To close a long use sell; to close a short use buy.

ratio_quantity.description: Contracts for this leg per unit of the order's quantity. Defaults to 1.

option_quotes.description: Live option quote(s) for the legs; same shape as get_option_quotes quotes[].

adjusted_mark_price.description: Mark adjusted for corporate actions on the underlying. Use against historical cost basis.

ask_price.description: Lowest current sell-side price. Zero when the book is closed or unavailable.

bid_price.description: Highest current buy-side price. Zero when the book is closed or unavailable.

break_even_price.description: Underlying price at which the position breaks even at expiration (strike +/- premium).

chance_of_profit_long.description: Model probability the long side ends in profit at expiration, decimal.

chance_of_profit_short.description: Model probability the short side ends in profit at expiration, decimal.

delta.description: Sensitivity of option price to a $1 move in the underlying.

gamma.description: Rate of change of delta per $1 move in the underlying.

high_fill_rate_buy_price.description: Upper bound of the likely fast-fill range for a buy.

high_fill_rate_sell_price.description: Upper bound of the likely fast-fill range for a sell.

instrument_id.description: UUID of the option contract; the canonical match key against closes[].instrument_id.

low_fill_rate_buy_price.description: Lower bound of the likely fast-fill range for a buy.

low_fill_rate_sell_price.description: Lower bound of the likely fast-fill range for a sell.

mark_price.description: Mid-market fair value — canonical current price for valuation.

open_interest.description: Outstanding contracts not yet closed or expired.

previous_close_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD trading date that previous_close_price corresponds to.

previous_close_price.description: Quote-derived prior close — best-effort, not the official settled close.

rho.description: Sensitivity to a 1% change in interest rates.

theta.description: Daily time decay ($/day); negative for long positions.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the quote refresh.

vega.description: Sensitivity to a 1% change in implied volatility.

order_checks.description: Pre-trade validation result. An empty object {} means the order is clean and safe to place. Non-empty carries alertType (the issue) plus alert-specific details.

alertType.description: Identifies the pre-trade-check issue. Surface verbatim to the user along with details.

details.description: Alert-specific structured detail payload. Shape varies by alertType (e.g. deposit_amount, leg list, timing info).

price.description: Echoed limit price per contract. Omitted for market/stop_market.

quantity.description: Echoed contract count.

stop_price.description: Echoed stop trigger price per contract. Omitted for limit/market.

type.description: Echoed user-facing order type (limit / market / stop_limit / stop_market).

guide.description: This tool does NOT place the order — it returns a preview for the user to review. Reaching this tool means the user opted into the review step, so you MUST present the preview to the user (legs, type, quantity, price/stop_price, alerts, fees, collateral, quote) and get explicit confirmation before calling place_option_order (for a new order) or replace_option_order (when modifying an existing order). This holds even when order_checks is empty ({}) — empty means no broker alerts, NOT that confirmation can be skipped; the user asked to review, so let them review. Non-empty order_checks: present alertType + details verbatim and ask the user to confirm before proceeding. On user ack, call place_option_order (or replace_option_order) with the same parameters. Use option_quotes (mark_price, bid/ask, greeks) to help judge cost. When fees is present, lead with total_fee; mention gold_fee_savings when non-zero. When collateral is present, show cash amount/direction; warn on cash.infinite=true (unlimited-risk position) or non-empty equities (share-based collateral). Echo the order type: for market/stop_market remind the user the fill price is unbounded; for stop_limit/stop_market note the order only triggers when the underlying reaches stop_price.

tools.name: run_scan
tools.description:
Execute a saved scanner (also called screener) and return live market results. A scan's filters are evaluated against current market data at request time — results are real-time, not cached.

Returns the scan's title, the total number of matching instruments, a list of instrument rows (with ticker, instrument_id, type, and one cell per visible column), plus the active sort and filters. The agent should present results as a table and mention this is live data.

Parameters:
- scan_id (required) — the scan identifier from get_scans or create_scan. Returns an error if the scan does not exist or does not belong to the calling user.

Use get_scans first to discover available scan_ids if the user has not specified one.

scan_id.description: The scan identifier to execute. Get this from get_scans or create_scan.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

result.description: Live scan results: matched instruments, total count, and the scan's metadata.

cortex_managed.description: True when this scan was created or modified by Cortex. Read-only via MCP tools.

filters_applied.description: Filters in effect when the scan was run. Always populated on create_scan / update_scan_filters responses; optional on run_scan.

filter_type.description: Filter category in human form (e.g. "RSI", "Volume", "Market Cap"). See the scanner-filter-specs resource for the full list.

interval.description: Time granularity for time-series filters (e.g. "1d", "1h"). Empty for snapshot filters.

length.description: Lookback length for filters that need one (e.g. RSI period of 14). Zero/omitted for filters that don't take a length.

plot.description: Plot / price-field input for filters that have one (e.g. % Change uses "open" or "close" to choose changeFromOpen vs changeFromClose). Empty for filters that don't take a plot.

values.description: Threshold values for the predicate. Single-element for >/</=, two-element for BETWEEN, multi-element for IN_LIST/ANY_OF.

results.description: Matched instruments, in the scan's current sort order. Live at time of execution.

columns.description: Column display name -> cell value, for every visible column in the scan's configuration. Numeric values are upstream-formatted strings; the agent should re-format for the user.

instrument_id.description: Robinhood instrument UUID. Pass to other trading tools that take an instrument_id.

instrument_type.description: Asset class: STOCK, ETF, etc. Mirrors hippo.model.v1.InstrumentType.

ticker.description: Trading symbol (e.g. AAPL). Sourced from the scan's symbol column; empty when the scan has no symbol column visible.

scan_id.description: Scan identifier. Stable across reads and writes.

sorted_by.description: Current sort column + direction in human form (e.g. "Volume desc"). Empty when no sort is configured.

total_items.description: Total instruments matching the scan's filters. May exceed len(results) when the scan's frontend_limit is hit.

guide.description:
How to interpret the response:
- result.total_items is the total number of instruments matching the scan's filters. result.results may contain fewer entries if the scan's frontend_limit caps the rows — mention this to the user when total_items > len(results).
- result.results is an array of matching instruments. Each entry has:
  - ticker: trading symbol (e.g. "AAPL"). Empty when the scan does not have a Symbol/Ticker column visible — use instrument_id then.
  - instrument_id: Robinhood instrument UUID. Pass this to other trading tools that take an instrument_id.
  - instrument_type: STOCK, ETF, etc.
  - columns: map of column display name -> cell value. Values are pre-formatted strings from upstream — re-format only if needed for the user.
- result.sorted_by tells you the row order (e.g. "Volume desc"). Mention it when describing the table.
- result.filters_applied lists the filters in effect — useful for confirming "this scan currently filters by RSI > 70".
- result.cortex_managed: true means the scan was created by Cortex inside Legend. Reads are fine, but update_scan_filters / update_scan_config will reject Cortex-managed scans — tell the user that if they ask to modify it.

When presenting results:
- Render as a table with ticker as the leftmost column followed by the most user-relevant columns (% Change, Volume, etc.).
- State total_items so the user knows how big the match set is.
- Note that results are live as of execution time — they will differ if run again later.
- If results is empty: tell the user the scan returned 0 matches right now. Suggest relaxing filters with update_scan_filters if they want broader results.

Error handling:
- "scan does not exist or you do not have access to it" → the scan_id is wrong or the scan belongs to a different user. Suggest get_scans to see available scans.
- Rate-limited or upstream failure → surface the error verbatim, no automatic retry.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Resolve a natural-language query to Robinhood instruments (stocks/ETFs), crypto pairs, or market indexes. Use when the user names an asset by name (or partial name) instead of a ticker/pair/index symbol, or when you need an instrument_id / currency-pair UUID / market-index id for a downstream tool. Defaults to instrument search; pass asset_type="currency_pair" for crypto or asset_type="market_index" for indexes (SPX, NDX, DJI, etc.). Instrument results carry symbol + instrument_id (use with get_equity_quotes / get_equity_tradability / place_equity_order or any instrument_id-based tool). Crypto results carry hyphenated symbol (e.g. BTC-USD) + id — the symbol routes to crypto quote/order tools, the id routes to watchlist tools as currency_pair_ids. Market-index results carry symbol + id — pass id to index quote tools for current values, or in the index_ids array of watchlist tools.

asset_type.description: Asset category to search. Supported: "instrument" (US-listed stocks/ETFs), "currency_pair" (crypto pairs like BTC-USD), and "market_index" (e.g. SPX, NDX, DJI). Defaults to "instrument" when omitted. More categories (events, futures) will be added as their corresponding tools land.

limit.description: Max results to return. Defaults to 10; clamped to 20.

query.description: Natural-language search query: company name, partial name, or ticker (e.g. "apple", "tesla motors", "AAPL"). Required.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

currency_pairs.description: Matched crypto pairs in upstream relevance order. Populated when asset_type=currency_pair; absent for instrument searches. Each entry carries the id (pass in the currency_pair_ids array of watchlist tools) and the hyphenated symbol (e.g. BTC-USD; pass to crypto quote/order tools).

id.description: Currency-pair UUID. Pass in the currency_pair_ids array of watchlist tools, or match against the currency_pair_id field on crypto position rows. (Use the symbol, not this id, with crypto quote/order tools.)

symbol.description: Canonical hyphenated symbol (e.g. 'BTC-USD'). Pass to crypto quote/order tools.

market_indexes.description: Matched market indexes in upstream relevance order. Populated when asset_type=market_index. Each entry carries id + symbol + name (e.g. SPX, NDX, DJI); pass id to index quote tools for live values, or use symbol to cross-reference index snapshot tools.

description.description: Longer human-readable description when upstream provides one. May be empty.

id.description: Market-index UUID. Pass to index quote tools for the current level, or in the index_ids array of watchlist tools.

symbol.description: Index ticker (e.g. 'SPX', 'NDX', 'DJI'). Cross-references the symbol on index snapshot tools.

results.description: Matched instruments in upstream relevance order. Empty when no Robinhood-tradable instrument matches the query.

instrument_id.description: Robinhood instrument UUID. Pass to tools that take an instrument_id.

name.description: Full instrument name, e.g. "Apple Inc. - Common Stock".

simple_name.description: Short product name shown in UI, e.g. "Apple". Omitted when upstream did not return one.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol, e.g. AAPL. Pass to tools that take a symbol (get_equity_quotes, get_equity_tradability, place_equity_order).

guide.description: Exactly one of results[] (instruments), currency_pairs[] (crypto), or market_indexes[] (indexes) is populated, matching the requested asset_type. Instrument entries carry symbol + instrument_id — pass symbol to symbol-based tools (get_equity_quotes, get_equity_tradability, place_equity_order) and instrument_id to id-based tools. Currency-pair entries carry hyphenated symbol (e.g. BTC-USD) + id — the symbol routes to crypto quote/order tools, the id routes to watchlist tools as currency_pair_ids. Market-index entries carry symbol + id — pass id to index quote tools for live values; the symbol cross-references index snapshot tools. Multiple results means the query was ambiguous (e.g. "apple" could match AAPL or APLE; "bitcoin" could match BTC-USD or BCH-USD); ask the user to disambiguate before placing trades. An empty result list means no Robinhood-tradable match — do not invent a symbol.

tools.name: unfollow_watchlist
tools.description: Stop following a Robinhood-curated list. The list itself is unchanged — it just no longer appears in the user's watchlists. Confirm with the user before calling.

list_id.description: UUID of the Robinhood-curated list to unfollow.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

follower.description: The new or pre-existing follower record. Omitted for unfollow which has no response body.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the user first followed this list. The follower endpoint is idempotent — re-following an already-followed list returns the existing record, so this timestamp may be older than 'just now'.

list_id.description: UUID of the list that was followed.

owner_type.description: Always 'robinhood' for follow_watchlist — only Robinhood-curated lists are followable.

user_id.description: UUID of the user that followed the list (always the caller).

status.description: 'ok' when the upstream accepted the operation.

guide.description: 404 means the user wasn't following that list (already-clean state).

tools.name: update_scan_config
tools.description:
Change the sort order of a saved scan's results table. The scan's filters and columns are preserved; only the sort changes.

Parameters:
- scan_id (required) — the scan to modify.
- sorting_column (required) — display name of the column to sort by. Must match a visible column on the scan; the error response lists available columns when no match is found.
- sorting_direction (required) — "asc" or "desc".

Restrictions:
- Cortex-managed scans (cortex_managed: true in get_scans) are rejected.
- v1 supports only sort changes — column add/remove/visibility/reorder are not exposed by this tool.

Returns the scan with the new sort applied and fresh live results in the new order.

scan_id.description: The scan to modify. Get this from get_scans or create_scan. Cortex-managed scans are rejected.

sorting_column.description: Display name of the column to sort by (e.g. "Volume", "% Change", "RSI"). Must match a column currently visible on the scan — call get_scans / run_scan first to see available columns.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

result.description: The scan after the sort change, with fresh live results in the new order.

cortex_managed.description: True when this scan was created or modified by Cortex. Read-only via MCP tools.

filters_applied.description: Filters in effect when the scan was run. Always populated on create_scan / update_scan_filters responses; optional on run_scan.

filter_type.description: Filter category in human form (e.g. "RSI", "Volume", "Market Cap"). See the scanner-filter-specs resource for the full list.

interval.description: Time granularity for time-series filters (e.g. "1d", "1h"). Empty for snapshot filters.

length.description: Lookback length for filters that need one (e.g. RSI period of 14). Zero/omitted for filters that don't take a length.

plot.description: Plot / price-field input for filters that have one (e.g. % Change uses "open" or "close" to choose changeFromOpen vs changeFromClose). Empty for filters that don't take a plot.

values.description: Threshold values for the predicate. Single-element for >/</=, two-element for BETWEEN, multi-element for IN_LIST/ANY_OF.

results.description: Matched instruments, in the scan's current sort order. Live at time of execution.

columns.description: Column display name -> cell value, for every visible column in the scan's configuration. Numeric values are upstream-formatted strings; the agent should re-format for the user.

instrument_id.description: Robinhood instrument UUID. Pass to other trading tools that take an instrument_id.

instrument_type.description: Asset class: STOCK, ETF, etc. Mirrors hippo.model.v1.InstrumentType.

ticker.description: Trading symbol (e.g. AAPL). Sourced from the scan's symbol column; empty when the scan has no symbol column visible.

scan_id.description: Scan identifier. Stable across reads and writes.

sorted_by.description: Current sort column + direction in human form (e.g. "Volume desc"). Empty when no sort is configured.

total_items.description: Total instruments matching the scan's filters. May exceed len(results) when the scan's frontend_limit is hit.

guide.description:
How to interpret the response:
- result.sorted_by — confirms the new sort ("Volume desc" etc.).
- result.results — live results re-ordered by the new sort.
- The change is persisted: subsequent run_scan calls on this scan_id will return rows in the new order.

When presenting:
- Confirm the new sort order to the user.
- Render the re-sorted results as a table. Eyeball-verify the sort with the user if they care: the top rows should match the requested direction.
- Mention that the sort is saved for future runs of the scan.

Error handling:
- "no column named X" → the requested sorting_column does not match any visible column. The error message lists available columns — relay them to the user and ask which they meant.
- "created by Cortex" → the scan is read-only via MCP. Suggest creating a fresh scan via create_scan.
- "scan X does not exist or you do not have access" → bad scan_id. Suggest get_scans.

tools.name: update_scan_filters
tools.description:
Replace the filters on an existing saved scan. The complete filter set provided replaces whatever filters the scan had — this is REPLACE semantics, not merge. To add a single filter to an existing scan, the agent must first read the scan (via get_scans or run_scan), then call this tool with all the existing filters plus the new one.

Parameters:
- scan_id (required) — the scan to modify. Get from get_scans or create_scan.
- filters (required) — the complete new filter set. Send [] to clear all filters. Each filter has filter_type (FILTER_TYPE_... enum), predicate, values, optional interval/length. Call get_scanner_filter_specs for valid combinations.

Restrictions:
- Cortex-managed scans (cortex_managed: true in get_scans) are rejected with a user-friendly error.
- Returns an error and does not apply any change if any filter fails validation.

Returns the scan with its new filters and fresh live results.

filters.description: The complete set of filters the scan should have after the update. REPLACE semantics — to add a filter, supply all existing filters plus the new one. To remove a filter, omit it from the array. To clear all filters, send []. Each filter: filter_type (FILTER_TYPE_... enum), predicate (>, <, =, BETWEEN, etc.), values, optional interval (e.g. 1d), optional length (e.g. 14 for RSI). Call get_scanner_filter_specs first for valid filter_type / predicate / interval / length combinations.

filter_type.description: Wire-format enum name, e.g. "FILTER_TYPE_RSI". See the scanner-filter-specs resource for valid values.

interval.description: Time granularity for time-series filters (e.g. "1d"). Use one of the supported_intervals from the filter's scanner-filter-specs entry.

length.description: Lookback length for filters that need one (e.g. RSI period of 14). Use one of the supported_lengths from the filter's scanner-filter-specs entry.

plot.description: Plot / price-field input for filters that have one (e.g. "open" or "close" for % Change). Use one of the supported_plots from the filter's scanner-filter-specs entry.

predicate.description: Wire-format enum name, e.g. "PREDICATE_GREATER_THAN". See the scanner-filter-specs resource for the predicates supported by each filter.

values.description: Threshold values. Single-element for unary predicates, two-element for BETWEEN, multi-element for IN_LIST/ANY_OF.

scan_id.description: The scan to modify. Get this from get_scans or create_scan. Cortex-managed scans (cortex_managed: true in get_scans output) are rejected.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

result.description: The scan after the update, plus fresh live results matching the new filter set.

cortex_managed.description: True when this scan was created or modified by Cortex. Read-only via MCP tools.

filters_applied.description: Filters in effect when the scan was run. Always populated on create_scan / update_scan_filters responses; optional on run_scan.

filter_type.description: Filter category in human form (e.g. "RSI", "Volume", "Market Cap"). See the scanner-filter-specs resource for the full list.

interval.description: Time granularity for time-series filters (e.g. "1d", "1h"). Empty for snapshot filters.

length.description: Lookback length for filters that need one (e.g. RSI period of 14). Zero/omitted for filters that don't take a length.

plot.description: Plot / price-field input for filters that have one (e.g. % Change uses "open" or "close" to choose changeFromOpen vs changeFromClose). Empty for filters that don't take a plot.

values.description: Threshold values for the predicate. Single-element for >/</=, two-element for BETWEEN, multi-element for IN_LIST/ANY_OF.

results.description: Matched instruments, in the scan's current sort order. Live at time of execution.

columns.description: Column display name -> cell value, for every visible column in the scan's configuration. Numeric values are upstream-formatted strings; the agent should re-format for the user.

instrument_id.description: Robinhood instrument UUID. Pass to other trading tools that take an instrument_id.

instrument_type.description: Asset class: STOCK, ETF, etc. Mirrors hippo.model.v1.InstrumentType.

ticker.description: Trading symbol (e.g. AAPL). Sourced from the scan's symbol column; empty when the scan has no symbol column visible.

scan_id.description: Scan identifier. Stable across reads and writes.

sorted_by.description: Current sort column + direction in human form (e.g. "Volume desc"). Empty when no sort is configured.

total_items.description: Total instruments matching the scan's filters. May exceed len(results) when the scan's frontend_limit is hit.

guide.description:
How to interpret the response:
- result.filters_applied lists the filters that are now in effect on the scan. Confirm this matches what the user requested.
- result.total_items reflects matches under the new filter set — tighter filters typically lower this count.
- result.results is fresh live data evaluated against the new filters.

When presenting:
- Confirm exactly which filters are now active (filters_applied) — useful especially after add/remove operations where the user wants to know the new total filter set.
- Mention the new total_items so the user sees the impact of the change.
- Render the new results as a table.
- If results is empty: tell the user no instruments match the new filter set. Suggest relaxing one filter (and offer to call update_scan_filters again with the relaxed set).

REPLACE-semantics callout:
- If the user said "add a market cap filter" but the request payload only had the new filter (not the existing ones), the existing filters were dropped because update_scan_filters REPLACES the filter set. Surface this if the result's filters_applied is shorter than expected. Recovery: read the scan via get_scans/run_scan, then call again with the full intended set.

Error handling:
- "filter[N]: ..." → validation failure at filter index N. Tell the user which filter and why.
- "created by Cortex" → the scan is read-only via MCP. Suggest creating a fresh scan via create_scan as the alternative.
- "scan X does not exist or you do not have access" → bad scan_id. Suggest get_scans.

tools.name: update_watchlist
tools.description: Rename a custom watchlist or change its icon/description. Robinhood-curated lists cannot be renamed; the call will fail with 404. Provide at least one of display_name, icon_emoji, display_description.

list_id.description: UUID of the watchlist to update. Obtain from get_watchlists.

data.description: Tool-specific result payload; see the inner properties for fields.

watchlist.description: The newly created or updated watchlist.

allowed_object_types.description: Which kinds of objects can be added to this list. Common values: instrument (US stock/ETF), currency_pair (crypto), index (market index), option_strategy (options watchlist — see add_option_to_watchlist), futures. add_to_watchlist supports instrument, currency_pair, and index; options use the dedicated add_option_to_watchlist tool; futures still require the Robinhood app.

display_description.description: Optional short description shown under the name.

icon_emoji.description: Emoji shown alongside the name. Present on custom lists; absent on Robinhood-curated lists.

id.description: List UUID. Pass to other watchlist tools (get_watchlist_items, add/remove_from_watchlist, update_watchlist) as list_id.

item_count.description: Number of items in the list.

owner_type.description: 'custom' means a user-created list. 'robinhood' means a curated list the user is following. Use this to distinguish user-owned lists (writable: rename, delete items, add items) from curated lists (read + follow/unfollow only).

guide.description: On success the response contains the full updated list. 404 means the list_id is not user-writable (it's curated or doesn't exist).
